title: Gate-2 (ii): DATA write-lane — gate_data_edit hook (content-bound sha256, one-shot) + gate_kb_edit DATA skip + registration
description:
> **Before Starting:** Groom right before doing it (Policy 8). This is **RQ-E ticket (ii)** of ADR-026; ticket (i) is the sibling **wh-hxt.5** (mints `evals/data-verdict.json`). The design is DECIDED in `docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md` RQ-B (`:170-265`) + RQ-E (ii) (`:350-389`) — QA/SEC-reviewed in-wave (SEC-Q2 content-binding/one-shot, SEC-Q3 hard ordering, QA-#2 DATA_SEGMENTS scope). **Conform, do not redesign.**

## Goal / Problem

The outer loop's only KB/DATA write gate today is `gate_kb_edit.py`, which admits any write under `/_shared/reference/` on a single `evals/gate-verdict.json==KEEP` (`gate_kb_edit.py:40-49`). That KEEP is earned by a corpus-scored KB change — but a watchlist **DATA row has no corpus-measurable merit**, so reusing the eval-J KEEP would be a **false-merit merge**. The watchlist's pinned home — `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/known-compromised.osv.json` (ADR-026 §3) — falls inside that wrongly-gated segment. Worse, `gate_kb_edit` checks ONLY `exists()`+`KEEP` — no content binding, no consume — so even the right verdict kind is replayable (TOCTOU: a KEEP for candidate A admits candidate B).

This ticket builds the **Gate-2 DATA write-lane**: `gate_data_edit.py` (PreToolUse) admits a write to a named DATA path ONLY when `evals/data-verdict.json` is KEEP, `path` matches, AND `sha256` matches a fresh recompute of the proposed write bytes — then **consumes** the verdict (one-shot). `gate_kb_edit` gets a tested DATA_PATHS skip so DATA writes are not double-gated.

## Background / Context

- ADR-026 §3 (the verdict mechanism: content-bound sha256, one-shot, named DATA paths, hard ordering) · ADR-024 §5 (four gates never merged) + §8 (hooks out-of-lane for the outer loop).
- teampcp doc RQ-B `:170-265` (incl. the HARD ORDERING block `:257-265`) + RQ-E (ii) `:350-389` + coordination `:391-406`.
- Code mirrored/modified (verified): `gate_kb_edit.py` GATED_SEGMENTS `:18`, `_is_gated_path :22-24`, `_edits_kb :27-34`, verdict read `:40-49`, `main()` exit-2 `:52-62` · `gate_kb_edit.sh:4` wrapper shape · `hooks.json:3-28` (PreToolUse `"Bash|Write|Edit"` matcher `:5`; gate_kb_edit.sh `:24`) · `tests/test_gate_kb_edit.py` `_verdict`/`_write` helpers `:12-18`, both-direction pins `:21-32`, exit codes `:62-69` · `tests/test_hooks_json.py` `test_registers_* :63-69`, executable-resolution `:107-113` · `confine_self_writes.py:40` (`/_shared/reference/` already in ALLOW_SEGMENTS — this ticket does NOT touch confine).
- Sibling **wh-hxt.5** mints the verdict shape `{verdict,path,sha256,validated}`; tests here construct that JSON as fixtures, so this ticket executes self-contained even before wh-hxt.5 merges.
- Baseline: hooks suite **121 passed** (grows with wh-hxt.5 + this ticket; stays green).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

1. **RED** — failing tests: `test_gate_data_edit.py` (the full matrix below) + the `DATA_PATHS` skip both-ways in `test_gate_kb_edit.py` + registration in `test_hooks_json.py`. Commit: `test: failing tests for gate_data_edit content-bound one-shot DATA gate`
2. **GREEN** — `gate_data_edit.py` + `gate_data_edit.sh` + the gate_kb_edit skip + hooks.json registration. Suite green. Commit: `feat: gate_data_edit DATA write-lane gate (ADR-026 ticket ii)`
3. **REFACTOR** — no single-use abstraction (Policy 2); stdlib-only. Commit: `refactor: tidy gate_data_edit / gate_kb_edit DATA skip`

## Steps

1. **`gate_data_edit.py`** (NEW, mirrors gate_kb_edit structure; stdlib-only): scoped by **exact normalized-path SUFFIX** (not substring — a `…known-compromised.osv.json.tmp` sibling must NOT match) to `DATA_SEGMENTS = ("/_shared/reference/known-compromised.osv.json",)` — initially ONLY the watchlist path (QA-#2; NO placeholder sidecar entry; wh-hxt.4 adds its path WITH a test). On Write/Edit to a DATA path: read `evals/data-verdict.json` under `event["cwd"]`; **fail-closed** (missing/empty/unparseable → exit 2); require verdict==KEEP AND path match AND sha256 == fresh recompute of the proposed bytes — Write: sha256(tool_input.content); **Edit: sha256 of the POST-EDIT bytes** (apply old_string→new_string to the on-disk file, hash the result — genuinely NEW logic, not in gate_kb_edit); any mismatch → exit 2 (SEC-Q2). **One-shot:** delete `evals/data-verdict.json` on a successful admit (fallback if a durability hazard surfaces in tests: record the consumed sha256 — note the choice in the commit body); replay with the same verdict → BLOCKED. Non-DATA paths → allow (out of scope). `main()`: unparseable stdin → 0; block → `[gate_data_edit] BLOCKED: …` stderr + exit 2.
2. **`gate_data_edit.sh`** (NEW): `exec python3 "$(dirname "$0")/gate_data_edit.py"`, chmod +x.
3. **`gate_kb_edit.py`** (EDIT, surgical): a local `DATA_PATHS` tuple (same suffix; deliberately duplicated, NOT cross-imported — independent hooks; comment cross-refs ADR-026 §3) checked BEFORE the GATED_SEGMENTS substring check → DATA paths return not-KB-gated.
4. **`hooks.json`** (EDIT): register `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/gate_data_edit.sh` in the existing PreToolUse `"Bash|Write|Edit"` group after gate_kb_edit.sh, `timeout: 600`.
5. **Tests**: `test_gate_data_edit.py` (new) + extend `test_gate_kb_edit.py` and `test_hooks_json.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria

Runner: `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests -q`. Pin BOTH directions per invariant (Policy 9).

- [ ] **Happy path (both):** DATA write with NO data-verdict → BLOCKED (2); with KEEP + path match + sha256(content) match → ALLOWED.
- [ ] **No eval-J reuse (false-merit closure):** DATA write with ONLY `gate-verdict.json==KEEP` on disk → BLOCKED.
- [ ] **KB lane unaffected (other direction):** KB-path write with ONLY a `data-verdict.json` on disk → still BLOCKED by gate_kb_edit.
- [ ] **Content binding (SEC-Q2, both):** KEEP minted for content A + Write of content B → BLOCKED; Write of content A → ALLOWED.
- [ ] **Edit post-edit-byte binding (both):** Edit whose post-edit bytes hash == verdict sha256 → ALLOWED; differing → BLOCKED.
- [ ] **One-shot / no replay:** after a successful consume, a second Write reusing the same verdict → BLOCKED.
- [ ] **Fail-closed:** missing/empty/unparseable data-verdict on a DATA write → BLOCKED.
- [ ] **DATA_SEGMENTS scope (QA-#2):** test asserts len(DATA_SEGMENTS)==1 and the wh-hxt.4 sidecar path is ABSENT.
- [ ] **Suffix not substring:** `…/known-compromised.osv.json.tmp` is NOT treated as the DATA path (test).
- [ ] **gate_kb_edit skip (both ways):** with a KEEP eval-J verdict on disk — DATA-path write NOT admitted/gated by gate_kb_edit; KB-path write still KB-gated.
- [ ] **HARD ORDERING (SEC-Q3/QA-#1):** test asserts a watchlist-path write is governed by gate_data_edit (DATA verdict), not gate_kb_edit, once registered. RELEASE-GATE NOTE: this ticket MUST merge + register BEFORE `known-compromised.osv.json` ever exists (wh-k6l / the feeder); a premature file blocks on the absent eval-J verdict or is admitted by an unrelated KB KEEP — the false-merit merge.
- [ ] **main() exit codes:** blocked → 2; allowed → 0; unparseable stdin → 0.
- [ ] **Registration:** test_hooks_json asserts gate_data_edit.sh registered + resolves to an executable script.

## Quality Gates (must pass before bd close)

```bash
nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker
```
Lint/type/coverage: N/A (per-package hooks-suite convention; no src/ tree).

## QA Before Push

- [ ] Happy path; edge (empty/unparseable verdict · A-verdict/B-write · replay · Edit post-edit path · eval-J-only DATA write); error handling (missing cwd/tool_input/content → fail-closed block, never a 0-returning traceback); no regressions (full hooks suite; KB lane behavior unchanged except the tested skip).

## Commit Message Format

`test:` / `feat:` / `refactor:` — author Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>; no AI attribution; operator-gated push.

## Risks / Dependencies

**Files to Create (3):** `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/gate_data_edit.py` · `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/gate_data_edit.sh` · `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_gate_data_edit.py`
**Files to Modify (4):** `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/gate_kb_edit.py` · `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_gate_kb_edit.py` · `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/hooks.json` · `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_hooks_json.py`
**NOTHING else** (no validator changes = wh-hxt.5; no data/schema files; no confine_self_writes edit — `/_shared/reference/` already in ALLOW_SEGMENTS).
**Depends on:** wh-hxt.5 (the verdict shape; fixtures make this ticket executable standalone).
**Blocks:** wh-k6l (first watchlist writes) · the wh-5es feeder impl. (wh-hxt.4 the SPIKE proceeds on ADR-026; its sidecar-path addition to DATA_SEGMENTS lands inside wh-hxt.4's impl with its own test.)
**Rollback:** delete the 3 new files; revert the gate_kb_edit skip + hooks.json hunks. No data to unwind.

## Notes / Open Questions

- The Edit content recompute (post-edit bytes) is NEW logic (gate_kb_edit never reconstructs content) — keep stdlib (`hashlib`), read the on-disk file under event cwd.
- One-shot delete vs invalidate: default DELETE on successful admit; fallback = record consumed sha256 (note in commit body). Replay test green either way.
- DATA_PATHS duplication across the two hooks is intentional (independent PreToolUse entries; no cross-import coupling); revisit only at a 3rd consumer.
- References: ADR-026 §3 · ADR-024 §5/§8 · teampcp RQ-B/RQ-E(ii)/coordination · SEC-Q2 · SEC-Q3 · QA-#1 · QA-#2.
close_reason: Gate-2 DATA write-lane: gate_data_edit (content-bound sha256, one-shot, fail-closed, normpath'd scope) + gate_kb_edit DATA skip + hooks.json registration. 3 security findings fixed + re-verified (HIGH-1 path-normalization //, HIGH-2 Bash channel, MED-3 malformed-verdict fail-open). hooks 123→182 +2 strict-xfail (>|/xargs residual → wh-hxt.15, wh-k6l blocker; mooted pre-wh-k6l by confine realpath + ADR-012). Builds against the real validator verdict shape. CLEAR-WITH-FIXES, ship-eligible.

title: Gate-2 (i): watchlist DATA validator — id-bound GHSA/OSV provenance + pinned watchlist-1.0 schema + regression-green (mints data-verdict.json; FROZEN)
description:
## Goal / Problem

The supply-chain **watchlist** (`known-compromised.osv.json`) and the tool-registry sidecar are **DATA**, not review-quality KB. The eval keep-or-revert gate (Gate-1) **structurally cannot** score a DATA edit — `evals/score.py:64-95` consumes only findings-vs-`label.json`, and a watchlist row has no corpus-measurable merit. Reusing an eval-J KEEP to admit a DATA row would be a **false-merit merge**. ADR-026 ratifies a SECOND deterministic gate — **Gate-2** — for watchlist/registry DATA.

This ticket builds the **input arm of Gate-2**: a pure-function validator (`_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py`) + the pinned `watchlist-1.0` JSON Schema, which together check **per-entry id-bound advisory provenance + schema validity + regression-green** and mint a SEPARATE, content-bound `evals/data-verdict.json`. The *enforcement* arm (the `gate_data_edit.py` PreToolUse hook that consumes that verdict) is **ticket (ii) — explicitly NOT this ticket**.

Outcome: a deterministic, gate-grade validator that the watchlist (wh-k6l), the OSSF feeder (wh-5es), and the registry sidecar + row-writer (wh-hxt.4 impl) all build to — schema-first, so it lands **before** any of them writes an entry.

## Background / Context

- **ADR-026** (`docs/ARD.md:415-521`) — ratified; the ONE watchlist-mechanism ADR. §1 the three Gate-2 checks (incl. the id-bound advisory-URL rule, SEC-Q4); §2 `watchlist-1.0` IS the OSV-superset that fulfils "OSV-schema"; §3 the verdict shape `{verdict,path,sha256,validated}`, content-bound + one-shot, and the SEC-Q12 verdict-writer-trust freeze of `validate_watchlist.py` + `watchlist-entry-schema.json` into `FROZEN_BASENAMES`.
- **Reviewed spec** — `docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md`: RQ-A (`:95-168`), RQ-C (`:267-288`), RQ-E draft ticket (i) (`:300-348`). QA- and security-reviewed in-wave; this is CONFORMANCE, not redesign.
- **Schema source of truth** — `docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md:59-81` (canonical example) + `:162-168` (RQ2 DECISION). Consumed, never re-litigated (ADR-026 §2).
- **Gate discipline** — ADR-024 §5 (four objects, four gates, never merged). Gate-2 is the DATA object.
- **Patterns (verified):** `validate_kb.py` (`SCHEMA_PATH :27`, `Draft202012Validator :38,82-89`, file/dir modes `:134-188`, exit codes `:191-209`); `_shared/scripts/validate_findings.py` (`:24,31-42,101-136` — the shared-validator precedent); `_shared/scripts/conftest.py:10-15` (sys.path shim → `import malware_db` with no cross-package install); `malware_db.py` (`load_malware_db :27` never-raises `:32`; `is_known_bad :48` EXACT-set `:53,59`; `_read_osv :62-68` values-only); `supply_chain.py:1042-1048` (version-aware S8 match); `confine_self_writes.py` (`FROZEN_BASENAMES :29`, checked `:69`; `/_shared/reference/` already in ALLOW_SEGMENTS `:40`).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)

1. **RED** — failing tests + neutralized OSV fixtures first: `test_validate_watchlist.py` (exit codes, id↔URL bind pass+fail, schema-invalid, --check-regression bad+clean, value-plane guard, verdict minting) + 2 confinement assertions in `test_confine_self_writes.py`. Suite FAILS (validator absent). Commit: `test: failing Gate-2 watchlist validator + confinement tests`
2. **GREEN** — minimal `validate_watchlist.py` + pinned schema + the FROZEN_BASENAMES hunk. All suites pass. Commit: `feat(_shared): Gate-2 watchlist DATA validator + pinned watchlist-1.0 schema`
3. **REFACTOR** — dedup vs validate_kb/validate_findings shapes; single source for the fixed reason vocabulary. Commit: `refactor: tidy Gate-2 validator reason vocabulary`

## Steps

1. **Pin `watchlist-1.0`** as a Draft-2020-12 JSON Schema at `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/watchlist-entry-schema.json` encoding the RQ2 DECISION shape exactly (`schema_version:"watchlist-1.0"`; `id`; REQUIRED `target ∈ {dependency,tool,extension}`; `affected[].package.{ecosystem,name}` mandatory + `versions`; `references[]` ≥1 with url; `extension.{marketplace,id,bad_versions}` OUTSIDE affected[]; `database_specific.{retrieved,watchlist_confidence}`). Do NOT re-decide the schema.
2. **Write `validate_watchlist.py`** mirroring `validate_kb.py`: `SCHEMA_PATH = parent.parent/"reference"/"watchlist-entry-schema.json"`; `load_schema()` → `validate_entry()` → `validate_file() -> (id, errors)` → `validate_dir()` with cross-file unique-id check. Pure stdlib + jsonschema; no LLM/RNG/network (Policy 5); reads OSV as values only.
3. **The three Gate-2 checks** (pure functions): **(1) id-bound provenance (SEC-Q4)** — ≥1 `references[].url` on the advisory host allow-list (`github.com/advisories/`, `osv.dev/`, `nvd.nist.gov/`, or project `github.com/<owner>/<repo>/security/advisories/GHSA-…`) AND the URL contains the entry's own `id`; the per-project branch additionally requires host==github.com AND parsed-advisory-id == entry.id. **(2) schema validity** against the pinned schema. **(3) regression-green** — `--check-regression` loads the candidate via `malware_db.load_malware_db` and asserts `is_known_bad` verdicts: bad version → True, clean sibling → False (exact-set, never substring).
4. **CLI + exit codes** (= validate_kb contract): `uv run --with jsonschema python validate_watchlist.py <file-or-dir> [--check-regression]`; 0 valid / 1 invalid (per-entry reason) / 2 usage.
5. **Mint `evals/data-verdict.json`** when ALL checks pass: `{"verdict":"KEEP"|"REVERT"|"INCONCLUSIVE","path","sha256":"<hex of EXACT validated bytes>","validated":"<iso8601>"}`. One-shot/content-bound CONSUMPTION is ticket (ii)'s `gate_data_edit.py` — this ticket MINTS only (docstring states the consumer).
6. **Value-plane guard (SEC-Q5):** fixed reason vocabulary (check name + offending key); feed-derived strings (urls, advisory prose) NEVER echoed to agent-facing stdout. Guard note: future `_shared/reference/*` enumeration must scope to `*.md`.
7. **Freeze the verdict-writer (SEC-Q12):** EDIT `confine_self_writes.py` — add BOTH `validate_watchlist.py` and `watchlist-entry-schema.json` to `FROZEN_BASENAMES` (`:29`) (option (a) of ADR-026 §3; mirrors keep_or_revert.py/score.py protection).
8. **Tests + neutralized fixtures** under `_shared/scripts/tests/`: valid entry; no-provenance; unrelated-id advisory URL; forged project-advisory; schema-invalid; bad-version + clean-sibling pair.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Exit codes (pin BOTH):** valid entry → 0; missing/ungrounded references[] → 1 with stated reason (assert ==0 AND ==1, NOT 0); no target arg → 2.
- [ ] **id↔URL binding (SEC-Q4, pin BOTH):** real-but-UNRELATED advisory url (host ok, id absent) → 1; forged `github.com/<owner>/<repo>/security/advisories/GHSA-fake` (id ≠ entry.id) → 1; own-id url → check passes. Fixture asserts "unrelated-but-real GHSA link → FAIL".
- [ ] **Schema validity:** bad `target` / missing `affected[].package.ecosystem` → 1 with offending key named.
- [ ] **Regression-green (exact-set):** bad version → is_known_bad True; clean sibling → False; pins != substring (db {"foo":{"1.2.3"}} does NOT flag "2.3").
- [ ] **Value-plane guard (SEC-Q5):** crafted token in references[].url does NOT appear in stdout (grep-assert absent).
- [ ] **Verdict minting:** data-verdict.json written ONLY when all three checks pass; sha256 == hashlib.sha256(exact validated bytes); shape exact.
- [ ] **Confinement (SEC-Q12, pin BOTH):** confine blocks Write/Edit to `validate_watchlist.py` AND `watchlist-entry-schema.json`; control still ALLOWS a normal `/_shared/reference/*.md` write. Hooks suite grows from the 121 baseline, stays green.
- [ ] **End-to-end:** `uv run --with jsonschema python plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py <fixture-dir> --check-regression` exits 0.
- [ ] **No scope creep:** git status shows ONLY the 4 created + 2 modified files. No gate_data_edit.py, no hooks.json, no gate_kb_edit edit, no watchlist data file.

## Quality Gates (must pass before bd close)

```bash
uv run --with pytest --with jsonschema pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/ -q
uv run --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests -q          # grows from 121
uv run --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q
uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .
uv run --with jsonschema python plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/fixtures --check-regression
```

## QA Before Push

- [ ] Happy path: valid neutralized entry → exit 0 + verdict minted.
- [ ] Edge: empty references[]; non-advisory host only; host ok id absent; forged project-advisory; target:extension with empty affected[]; dir vs file --check-regression.
- [ ] Errors: unparseable JSON → 1 (no crash); missing arg → 2; non-dict top-level → 1.
- [ ] No regressions: deps-scan S8 version-aware + malware_db suites unaffected.

## Commit Message Format

`test:` / `feat(_shared):` / `refactor:` — author Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>; no AI attribution; operator-gated push.

## Risks / Dependencies

**Files to Create (4):** `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/validate_watchlist.py` · `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/watchlist-entry-schema.json` · `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_validate_watchlist.py` · `plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/fixtures/`
**Files to Modify (2):** `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/confine_self_writes.py` (FROZEN_BASENAMES + the two basenames) · `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/tests/test_confine_self_writes.py`
**NOTHING else** (gate_data_edit.py / hooks.json / gate_kb_edit.py / any *.osv.json data file = ticket (ii) / wh-k6l).
**Depends on:** nothing open (ADR-026 ratified; wh-562 closed; jsonschema+pytest already declared).
**Blocks:** wh-k6l (watchlist file) · the wh-5es feeder impl · wh-hxt.4's row-writer IMPL (the wh-hxt.4 SPIKE itself consumes ADR-026 and is NOT blocked).
**Risk — HARD ORDERING (SEC-Q3):** the watchlist data file MUST NOT exist until ticket (ii) lands (gate_data_edit registered + gate_kb_edit DATA_PATHS skip) — this ticket creates no data file, preserving the ordering by scope.
**Risk — basename matching:** FROZEN_BASENAMES is basename-wide (`:69`); verify no legitimate same-named file elsewhere (none today).
**Rollback:** delete the 4 created files; revert the FROZEN_BASENAMES hunk + its test. No data migrations.

## Notes / Open Questions

- Self-contained: one developer subagent, no inter-agent messaging; all anchors file:line-cited.
- Encoded security findings: SEC-Q4 / SEC-Q5 / SEC-Q12 — reviewed in-wave; implement, do not re-debate.
- Schema FROZEN by reference (ADR-026 §2) — underspecification → follow-up against the schema doc, never fork the shape here.
- References: ADR-026 (docs/ARD.md:415-521) · teampcp doc RQ-A/RQ-C/RQ-E(i) · ADR-024 §5 · monitoring doc :59-81,:162-168.
notes: B1-REVIEW FINDING (2026-06-10, route here): the watchlist provenance allowlist must accept the OSV.dev malicious-packages feed + vendor-advisory hosts (socket.dev/stepsecurity.io), NOT GHSA-only — Hades (AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003, just shipped) has NO GHSA yet (20260610_hades_shai_hulud_pypi.md:92-95 Gate-2 note). ALSO fix the doc-vs-code gap white-hacker found: PRD.md:247 FR-16 VC asserts metadata.source must match AML.T/ASI/CVE — validate_kb does NOT enforce it and vendor-host provenance is now valid; soften the VC to 'advisory-ID OR allow-listed vendor host' as part of this Gate-2 provenance work.
close_reason: Gate-2 validator + pinned watchlist-1.0 schema. All ACs PASS; 3 security findings (HIGH-1 provenance substring-bypass, HIGH-2 schema-error injection-leak, MED-3 verdict TOCTOU) FIXED + adversarially re-verified CLEAR. validator 55 · _shared 149 · hooks 123 · manifest · plugin all green. Provenance: path-segment id-binding, github via canonical /advisories/<id> only, vendor/osv/nvd hosts (Hades no-GHSA case preserved); per-project branch DROPPED (self-mintable). AC7 (SAST flip) N/A here. Operator-gated: uncommitted.

title: Spike: ratify CONTAIN (assume-breach / zero-trust tool execution) as the PRIMARY supply-chain control — generalize the deps-scan sandbox into a shared tool-exec lane + CI hardening + ADR
description:
## Problem statement

The lifecycle epic (ADMIT→PIN/VERIFY→DIVERSIFY→MONITOR→RETIRE) is entirely SELECTION + VERIFICATION — every
stage answers "is this tool trustworthy?" But you CANNOT verify a tool/dependency is uncompromised, and 2026
proved verification-by-reputation is defeatable: Mini Shai-Hulud (May 2026; TanStack/Mistral/OpenSearch)
victims had VALID SLSA Build L3 provenance + OIDC trusted-publishing + 2FA and were STILL compromised — the
worm hijacked the LEGITIMATE pipeline. StepSecurity: "provenance confirms WHICH pipeline produced the
artifact, not WHETHER the pipeline was behaving as intended." The only control that stopped it in-flight was
an EGRESS ALLOWLIST. Tool diversity is the cited fix in ZERO 2026 postmortems. So swapping Trivy != security.
The durable control is CONTAINMENT: assume any tool IS backdoored and make the backdoor INERT by denying
network/egress + creds + host access. This is the 2026 consensus (NIST SSDF SP 800-218 / SP 800-204D;
CISA-NSA Defending CI/CD; OpenSSF S2C2F; SLSA L3 hermetic). white-hacker already has the SEED — the Agents
Rule of Two (agents/white-hacker.md:39-41) + the deps-scan-sandbox LOCKDOWN (docker/deps-scan-sandbox/run.sh:
--network none, --read-only+tmpfs, --cap-drop ALL, no-new-privileges, non-root, --pids/--memory, --rm). This
spike ELEVATES containment from a posture line + one-skill sandbox to the PRIMARY control wrapping the whole
lifecycle. Research grounding: docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (CONTAIN section) +
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G4 + LBC-4/LBC-5).

## ADR OWNERSHIP (settles the §9.3 collision — recorded 2026-06-09)
**THIS ticket owns THE single strategy ADR** (next-available number AT WRITE TIME — last written ADR-023;
do NOT hard-code): CONTAIN is the PRIMARY supply-chain control; the 5 lifecycle stages are defense-in-depth
under it; the CONTAIN invariant (>=2 of {network/egress, creds-in-env, host-write} ABSENT at every tool
execution) is the registry rule. wh-hxt.2 (runbook + diversity policy) REFERENCES this ADR — it does NOT
append a competing lifecycle ADR. The ADR should also CITE (not re-debate) ADR-007 (opt-in sandboxed PoC
detonation precedent), ADR-016 (defense-in-depth confinement precedent), ADR-003 (degrade-to-floor when
docker absent), ADR-006 (pin) — and CLARIFY, append-only, that ADR-015's "registry self-updates" sentence
is design intent until wh-hxt.4 lands, and that ADR-021's tag-pin lane must resolve tags to commit SHAs
(the force-push lesson) unless wh-562's Gate-2 ADR carries that supersession instead (exactly one of the
two states it; cross-reference from the other).

## Research questions (each -> a decision)
- RQ1 — The CONTAIN invariant: codify ">=2 of {network/egress, creds-in-env, host-write} ABSENT at every
  tool execution" as the registry rule. Which tools in the set (Grype/Syft, Checkov, OSV-Scanner,
  gitleaks, native gates) can run fully offline + no-creds today, and which need a fetch/analyze SPLIT
  (network-on fetch step, network-off analyze step — the pattern deps-scan-sandbox/fetch-snapshot.sh already
  uses)? Produce the per-tool containment matrix.
- RQ2 — Generalize the sandbox: should docker/deps-scan-sandbox/run.sh become a SHARED tool-execution lane
  (a _shared wrapper any capability calls) or stay per-skill with a shared LOCKDOWN[] array? Decide the shape
  that keeps ADR-003 graceful-degrade (no docker -> fall to the Read/Grep/Glob floor, tool_assisted:false).
- RQ3 — THE INNER-LOOP AUTO-ROUTE BRIDGE (promoted from notes — loop-leverage G4): the sandbox is OPT-IN
  today, invoked by NO inner-loop skill or the agent; at a default review S8 degrades to [] with "malware-db"
  in tools_unavailable (supply_chain.py:1122-1124; activation needs hand-passed malware_db, SKILL.md:82).
  Decide: when a pinned snapshot path is configured AND docker is available, auto-route the deps-scan stage
  through run.sh (S8 default-when-safe), else fall back to S1-S7 + tools_unavailable. Decide default-on vs
  louder-opt-in (the user may not want a surprise docker run). PREREQ: build-verify ./run.sh build &&
  ./run.sh test on a daemon host (README.md:87-88 authored-not-build-verified; bd memory records 67 passed
  on Rancher Desktop — re-confirm).
- RQ4 — ARTIFACT provenance-verify at admission (THIS ticket's arm — distinct from wh-562's Gate-2):
  full commit-SHA / image-digest / binary-checksum + cosign/SLSA VERIFY (not generate-and-trust) for the
  TOOLS the agent executes. This is CONTAIN's admission arm and operates on ARTIFACTS. wh-562's Gate-2 is a
  DIFFERENT mechanism for a DIFFERENT object kind: a deterministic DATA gate (per-entry GHSA/OSV provenance
  + OSV-schema + regression) for WATCHLIST/REGISTRY ENTRIES. Cross-reference; do NOT fold them into one
  mechanism (the 2026-06-09 audit corrected the earlier "one mechanism" framing — two object kinds, two gates).
- RQ5 — CI hardening guidance for .github/workflows/ + ci/security-review.action.yml: SHA-pin Actions (not
  tags — the trivy-action force-push), minimal GITHUB_TOKEN (contents:read per-job), OIDC scope-pinned to an
  immutable workflow + protected branch, EGRESS ALLOWLIST (Harden-Runner block mode — the control that
  stopped Mini Shai-Hulud), --ignore-scripts, ephemeral runners, atomic secret rotation. Produce a hardened-
  CI checklist (cite GitHub SHA-pin policy 2025-08-15; EU CRA reporting 2026-09-11 / SBOM-mandatory). Decide
  where it lives: ci/ docs vs ADR content (open decision §9.4).
- RQ6 — The reframe: confirm in the ADR that CONTAIN is PRIMARY and ADMIT/PIN/DIVERSIFY/MONITOR/RETIRE are
  defense-in-depth UNDER it (none survives an undetected compromise; CONTAIN does).

## Constraint (by design — do not "fix")
CONTAIN enforcement code is OUT-of-lane for the outer loop: confine_self_writes FROZEN/CONTROL basenames +
default-deny (:69-77) deny self-rewrite of hooks/gate/sandbox. Improvements arrive as human-PR'd TDD diffs,
never KB text. This asymmetry is identity preservation (Rule 5) — the ADR should state it.

## Investigation plan
1. Read docker/deps-scan-sandbox/{run.sh,fetch-snapshot.sh,README.md} + the deps-scan S8 offline path.
2. Read agents/white-hacker.md:39-41 (Rule of Two) + _shared/reference/tool-registry.md + detect_tools.py.
3. Build the per-tool containment matrix (RQ1) + decide the shared-lane shape (RQ2) + the auto-route bridge
   + its default (RQ3; build-verify run.sh first).
4. Specify the artifact-provenance admission arm (RQ4) — cross-referenced with wh-562's Gate-2, not merged.
5. Produce the hardened-CI checklist + decide its home (RQ5).
6. Draft THE strategy ADR (next-available number AT WRITE TIME) per the ADR-ownership block above.

## Exit criteria (we are done when)
- THE strategy ADR appended to docs/ARD.md (next-free # at write): CONTAIN primary; lifecycle =
  defense-in-depth; the CONTAIN invariant as registry rule; the ADR-015 design-intent + ADR-021 tag-pin
  clarifications carried here or explicitly delegated to wh-562's ADR.
- A per-tool containment matrix + the shared-tool-exec-lane decision (generalize sandbox vs shared LOCKDOWN).
- The auto-route bridge decision (S8 default-when-safe vs louder-opt-in) + run.sh build-verified on a
  daemon host (the prereq for relying on the lane).
- A hardened-CI checklist (SHA-pin / minimal token / OIDC scope / egress-allowlist / --ignore-scripts) +
  a decision on where it lives.
- Impl follow-up ticket(s): (a) the shared lane + live-verify ONE real tool (e.g. Grype) running fully
  offline + no-creds + sandboxed against a pinned snapshot; (b) the auto-route bridge; (c) the CI checklist
  wired into ci/security-review.action.yml. Even a "keep it per-skill" outcome must record the decision.

## Time box
~1-2 days. Research is largely done (the strategy CONTAIN section + the loop-leverage audit); this is
decision + ADR + impl-ticket carving.

## Likely outcomes
- CONTAIN ratified as primary; the deps-scan-sandbox LOCKDOWN lifted to a shared tool-exec lane; S8
  default-when-safe; a backdoored Grype/Checkov becomes inert.
- DIVERSIFY demoted from "the security answer" to blast-radius reduction (defense-in-depth).
- wh-562 stays a SEPARATE, re-scoped Gate-2 DATA-gate spike (cross-referenced, not folded).

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (CONTAIN section + 2026 sources: StepSecurity Mini
Shai-Hulud postmortem, SLSA L3 hermetic, NIST SP 800-218/204D, CISA-NSA Defending CI/CD, OpenSSF S2C2F,
GitHub SHA-pin policy 2025-08-15, EU CRA). docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G4,
LBC-4/5, §9 decisions). docker/deps-scan-sandbox/. ADR-001/003/006/007/015/016/019. Siblings: wh-562
(Gate-2 DATA gate — blocked by this), wh-nvk (DIVERSIFY), wh-xn0 (ADMIT), wh-5es (MONITOR), wh-d5b
(RETIRE interim), wh-hxt.2 (runbook — references this ADR), wh-hxt.4 (ADMIT-via-loop).
notes: GROOM 2026-06-10 (pre-claim pass; supersedes the 2026-06-09 loop-wiring note — all cites re-verified at HEAD 338d667): VERDICT READY with 3 corrections. (1) STALE CITE: the S8 degrade is now supply_chain.py:1271-1276 (unavailable.add('malware-db')), NOT :1122-1124 — wave-1a/0871c4f moved it; SKILL.md:81-82 activation cite still good; white-hacker.md:39-41 Rule-of-Two cite still good. (2) RQ3 PREREQ STATE CHANGED TWICE: the sandbox WAS docker-build-verified post-authoring (bd memory: Rancher Desktop/moby, docker 29.1.3, 67 passed 1 deselected) — BUT (a) that 67-count predates wave-1a; the deps-scan package is now 102 tests, so the image must be REBUILT + re-run (expect ~102) before relying on the lane; (b) docker is NOT currently reachable on this host — only the 'default' context exists pointing at /var/run/docker.sock (not exposed here; the rancher-desktop context is unregistered, i.e. Rancher Desktop not running). OPERATOR ACTION before RQ3's build-verify: start Rancher Desktop (re-registers the context), then ./run.sh build && ./run.sh test. RQ3's DESIGN work (auto-route decision) does NOT block on this — only the live verification does. (3) ALL else verified: sandbox files exist; ci/security-review.action.yml + .github/workflows/ci.yml exist (RQ5 targets); last written ADR = ADR-023 (claim holds, nothing reserved); all 7 cited ADRs resolve; no blockers; blocks wh-562 + wh-hxt.2 as designed. Spike = research+ADR+ticket-carving, no package code (no test baseline of its own; deps-scan baseline 102 green per wh-7o1 groom).
close_reason: Closed

title: EPIC: supply-chain-resilient tooling — a self-correcting capability lifecycle (admit→pin/verify→diversify→monitor→retire)
description:
## Goal
Govern white-hacker's capability TOOLS so that a COMPROMISE of any tool — Trivy, its replacement, or one we
have not picked yet — cannot harm us, EVEN in the window before the advisory drops. The load-bearing control
is CONTAINMENT (assume-breach / zero-trust execution): run every tool offline + no-creds + sandboxed +
provenance-verified, so a backdoor is INERT. ON TOP of that, a SELF-CORRECTING LIFECYCLE makes tool
compromise *and* staleness normal, gated, reversible events. Strategy:
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md · Loop-leverage audit + handoff:
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (the lifecycle RIDES the existing inner/outer
self-improvement loops — wire them, don't build bespoke machinery).

## Why (swapping is NOT the solution)
Trivy compromised (TeamPCP, CVE-2026-33634) + trivy-mcp unmaintained/stale-pinned. A point-swap is a
TREADMILL AND not security: you cannot know whether the alternatives are also compromised. Mini Shai-Hulud
(May 2026) victims had VALID SLSA Build L3 provenance + OIDC + 2FA and were STILL compromised; the only
control that stopped it in-flight was an EGRESS ALLOWLIST. Tool diversity is the cited fix in ZERO 2026
postmortems. CONTAINMENT (not a better tool list) is the durable answer.

## PRIMARY control: CONTAIN (assume-breach / zero-trust execution) → wh-hxt.3
The CONTAIN invariant: at least 2 of {network/egress, creds-in-env, host-write} ABSENT at every tool
execution. Seeds exist: the Agents Rule of Two (agents/white-hacker.md:39-41) + the deps-scan-sandbox
LOCKDOWN (docker/deps-scan-sandbox/run.sh). wh-hxt.3 ratifies THE strategy ADR, generalizes the sandbox to
a shared tool-exec lane, decides the S8 auto-route bridge (the sandbox is opt-in today — not invoked by any
inner-loop skill), and produces the hardened-CI checklist.

## SETTLED COORDINATION DECISIONS (2026-06-09 — children must honor these)
1. **ADR ownership (the §9.3 collision):** wh-hxt.3 owns THE single strategy ADR (CONTAIN-primary +
   lifecycle-as-defense-in-depth + the invariant). wh-hxt.2 references it (runbook + diversity policy — no
   competing ADR). wh-562 owns ONE watchlist-mechanism ADR (Gate-1 vs Gate-2 + schema + write-lane) — wh-5es's
   ADR deliverable FOLDS IN there. wh-xn0 (admissibility) + wh-nvk (drop-Trivy) keep their own. Six planned
   ADRs collapsed to FOUR; every number computed at write time (last written ADR-023; NOTHING reserved).
2. **TWO GATES, two object kinds:** Gate-1 = the eval keep/revert (KB/checklist text edits — what the corpus
   can score). Gate-2 = deterministic per-entry GHSA/OSV provenance + OSV-schema + regression (watchlist/
   registry DATA edits — the eval gate STRUCTURALLY cannot score these; reusing it = a false-merit merge).
   ARTIFACT provenance (tool binaries: SHA/digest/checksum+cosign) is a THIRD thing — wh-hxt.3's RQ4, not Gate-2.
3. **ONE watchlist schema:** the OSV-superset — wh-5es RQ2 FINALIZES it, wh-k6l INSTANTIATES the file,
   wh-562's Gate-2 VALIDATES it. No bespoke tool-keyed shape; tools are entries with target:tool.
4. **The registry rewrite is ONE coordinated change:** wh-xn0 (columns) + wh-nvk (rows) + wh-hxt.1 (health
   column) + wh-hxt.2 (policy text) all touch tool-registry.md + SCANNER_PREFERENCE — combined or explicitly
   sequenced after wh-nvk; never uncoordinated parallel writers.
5. **CONTAIN code is out-of-lane for self-edit** (confine FROZEN/CONTROL + default-deny) — improvements via
   human-PR'd TDD diffs, never KB text. By design (Rule 5); the strategy ADR states it.

## DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH: the 5-stage lifecycle (UNDER CONTAIN), riding the existing loops
1. **ADMIT** — license/egress/maintenance/integrity gates → wh-xn0 (+ staleness gate wh-hxt.1) + the
   ADMIT-via-loop proposer → **wh-hxt.4** (the registry-row writer; sec-kb-refresh primary arm — the
   "registry self-updates" promise of ADR-015, aspirational until this lands)
2. **PIN+VERIFY** — artifact provenance (SHA/digest/cosign) → wh-hxt.3 RQ4; watchlist-DATA integrity →
   wh-562 Gate-2
3. **DIVERSIFY** — blast-radius reduction, NOT prevention → wh-nvk (the replacement set) + the policy (wh-hxt.2)
4. **MONITOR** — the OSSF feeder into sec-kb-refresh (poll_feeds has NO OSSF parser today) + the S8
   version-aware fix → wh-5es (feeder + schema) + wh-4k9 (S8) + wh-k6l (the file) + staleness (wh-hxt.1)
5. **RETIRE+REPLACE** — quarantine→select→record→retire, reusable → wh-d5b (stopgap, iac-only edit) →
   wh-nvk (permanent) + the runbook (wh-hxt.2) + tool/watchlist retire paths (wh-hxt.1)

## Execution Waves (MANDATORY table — one wave = one `/launch-team <ids…>`; deps wired in bd)

| Wave | Ticket | One-line | Type | Blocked by |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | wh-4k9 * | S8 version-aware match (resolved-version extraction first) — do FIRST, smallest win | code | — |
| 1 | wh-hxt.3 | THE strategy ADR + sandbox→shared lane + S8 auto-route bridge + CI checklist | spike | — |
| 1 | wh-d5b | Trivy stopgap: the ONE iac reorder (detect_tools.py:116) + registry caveat | code | — |
| 1 | wh-xn0 | admissibility gates (license/egress) + registry-column spec | spike | — |
| 1 | wh-5es | OSSF feeder design + FINALIZE the OSV-superset schema | spike | — |
| 1 | wh-q86 * | KB AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 in a NEW supply-chain-2.md | doc | — |
| 2 | wh-562 | Gate-2 DATA gate + write-lane + the ONE watchlist-mechanism ADR | spike | wh-hxt.3 |
| 5 | wh-k6l * | the curated OSV watchlist file + loader-merge (in malware_db.py) + append process — path SETTLED `_shared/reference/known-compromised.osv.json` (ADR-026) | code | wh-4k9 ✓ · wh-hxt.5 · wh-hxt.6 (HARD ORDERING: gate registered BEFORE the file exists) |
| 2 | wh-nvk | replacement-set scorecard + permanent Trivy removal spec | spike | wh-d5b |
| 3 | wh-hxt.4 | ADMIT-via-loop: registry sidecar + arm-agnostic row writer | spike | wh-562 |
| 3 | wh-hxt.2 | retire→replace runbook + diversity-by-design policy (no ADR) | doc | wh-hxt.3, wh-nvk |
| 3 | wh-hxt.5 | Gate-2 (i): watchlist DATA validator + pinned watchlist-1.0 schema (mints data-verdict; FROZEN) | code | — (ADR-026 ratified; wh-562 ✓) |
| 3 | wh-hxt.7 | the ONE registry rewrite: ADR-025 columns + ADR-027 rows + SCANNER_PREFERENCE Trivy removal + lock-regex retirement | code | — (wh-xn0 ✓ · wh-nvk ✓ · wh-d5b ✓) |
| 4 | wh-hxt.6 | Gate-2 (ii): gate_data_edit hook (content-bound sha256, one-shot) + gate_kb_edit DATA skip + hooks.json registration | code | wh-hxt.5 |
| 4 | wh-hxt.1 | staleness gate/signal + tool/watchlist RETIRE paths | code | wh-hxt.7 (wired 2026-06-10) |

\* = hot-path sibling, not an epic child (wh-4k9, wh-k6l, wh-q86) — included because the waves are
launched as a program; they appear in exactly one wave each.

**Wave-validity caveats (coordination handoffs, per the epic template):** (a) Wave 1 has THREE
ADR-appending spikes (wh-hxt.3, wh-xn0; wh-5es feeds wh-562's) — `docs/ARD.md` is append-only and
numbers are taken at write time, so appends serialize trivially; do not parallel-edit the file itself.
(b) wh-d5b and wh-xn0 both reference tool-registry.md, but wh-d5b edits it while wh-xn0 only SPECS
columns (its impl lands with wh-nvk's rewrite) — disjoint in practice. (c) Launch each wave via
`/launch-team <ids…>`; end with `/handoff wave-N-wh-hxt`.

## Definition of Done
- THE strategy ADR ratified (CONTAIN primary; lifecycle defense-in-depth; the invariant as registry rule).
- The sandbox lane generalized + ONE real tool live-verified offline+no-creds+sandboxed; the S8 auto-route
  decision made (build-verified run.sh).
- Gate-2 exists (validator + write-lane); the ONE OSV-superset watchlist is seeded, fed (OSSF feeder), and
  version-aware (no name-only FP bomb).
- A hardened-CI checklist exists; ADMIT includes maintenance; RETIRE covers tools + watchlist entries;
  diversity-by-design stated with the honest blast-radius framing; the RETIRE→REPLACE runbook documented
  (Trivy worked through it).
- The registry self-update arm (wh-hxt.4) designed with its impl ticket carved.

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md · 20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (the
research+handoff record: leverage map, G1-G6, verified claims incl. the LBC-6 correction) ·
20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md · 20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md ·
ADR-001/002/003/006/007/015/016/019/022/023.

> **Wave addendum 2026-06-10 (post wave-2 keystone):** wh-hxt.5/.6/.7 created from the QA/SEC-reviewed RQ-E + RQ6 specs (ADR-026/027 impl). Wave 3 = wh-hxt.4 (spike doc) + wh-hxt.5 + wh-hxt.7 — file-disjoint, one /launch-team. wh-hxt.2 MOVED to wave 4 (groom 2026-06-10: its registry policy text overlaps wh-hxt.7's tool-registry.md — single-writer rule; bd dep wired), joining wh-hxt.6 + wh-hxt.1. wh-k6l moved to wave 5 (hard ordering via .5+.6, wired in bd). The SAST-default flip is a gated follow-up (lens-5, Java-inclusive re-baseline) — NOT scheduled.

title: Curated EXTENSIBLE known-compromised watchlist (git-tracked OSV, loader-merged, append-on-new-advisory) — seed with current entries
description:
## Goal
Build the EXTENSIBLE MECHANISM for a curated, git-tracked known-compromised watchlist that deps-scan's
S8 checks — NOT a one-off TeamPCP snapshot. The deliverable is the **format + the loader-merge + a
documented, human-gated APPEND process**, so EVERY future compromise (not just today's) is a small dated
entry someone can add in minutes. Seed it with the current confirmed entries as the FIRST batch.

## Why (re-scoped per review)
Hard-coding "the TeamPCP campaign" is too specific — compromises are continuous. The watchlist must be a
LIVING, append-only list with a clear "a new advisory dropped → add an entry" path. This ticket is the
manual-curated FOUNDATION; wh-5es generalizes it (extensions, digests, multi-surface) and AUTOMATES the
updates (the sec-kb-refresh OSSF feeder — note: that feeder is design-intent today, poll_feeds has no
OSSF parser; this ticket's manual append process is the ONLY working path until wh-5es lands). The
entries below are DATA/examples, not the point.

## COORDINATION (added 2026-06-09 — loop-leverage G2; agree BEFORE creating the file so it never moves)
- **Schema:** the file instantiates the OSV-superset that **wh-5es RQ2 finalizes** — plain OSV that
  load_malware_db (malware_db.py:26) already parses for the package kind; tools enter as entries with a
  target:tool tag (NOT a bespoke tool-keyed shape — that was dropped).
- **Gate:** every entry (seed batch included) must pass **wh-562's Gate-2 validator** — per-entry
  GHSA/OSV source URL + OSV-schema validity + regression-green.
- **Write-lane:** the planned home `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/` is NOT in
  confine_self_writes ALLOW_SEGMENTS (:40) — the outer loop cannot write there today. The path vs an
  ALLOW_SEGMENTS extension is **wh-562 RQ-B's decision** — confirm it before step 1.

## Prerequisites
- DEPENDS ON wh-4k9 (S8 version-aware match) — else popular packages FP-flag every user.
- load_malware_db (malware_db.py:26) parses OSV docs → {name:set[versions]}; OSV is the interop standard
  (GHSA / OSV.dev / OSSF all export it), so the curated file scales + the future automated feed appends
  in the SAME shape. Shares the Trivy entry with wh-562.

## Steps
1. Confirm the file path with wh-562 RQ-B (write-lane decision), THEN add the GENERICALLY-NAMED curated
   watchlist `known-compromised.osv.json` (or a small dir, one file per ecosystem) — OSV format; each
   entry dated + carrying its advisory source/url. NOT named after any one campaign.
2. Loader merge: load_malware_db (or the supply_chain.py caller) reads the curated file IN ADDITION to
   the optional big OSSF snapshot (union the maps); never-raise/degrade preserved.
3. Document the APPEND PROCESS + an entry TEMPLATE in reference/MALWARE-DB.md: "when a supply-chain
   compromise is confirmed (GHSA/OSV/OSSF), add a dated OSV entry via a human-gated draft-PR (ADR-012),
   Gate-2-validated: name, ecosystem, exact bad versions, source URL, retrieved date."
4. SEED the FIRST dated batch (2026-06, each sourced): litellm [1.82.7,1.82.8] (GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76);
   telnyx [4.87.1,4.87.2]; @tanstack/* (the compromised set — cite the postmortem); trivy [0.69.4]
   (GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23). Data, not logic.
5. Tests: the loader merges the curated file; a seeded bad version flags (no OSSF snapshot needed); a
   safe version does NOT (needs wh-4k9); **EXTENSIBILITY: a NEW fixture entry added to the file flags
   WITHOUT any code change.**

## Files to Modify/Create
- NEW plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/known-compromised.osv.json (path per wh-562 RQ-B)
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/malware_db.py (merge) or the caller
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/MALWARE-DB.md (the append process + template)
- tests

## Verification
- package tests green
- litellm==1.82.7 in a lockfile, NO OSSF snapshot → S8 HIGH supply-chain finding
- a SAFE version → no flag (relies on wh-4k9)
- extensibility test: a new (name, version) fixture entry flags with NO code edit
- the curated file validates as OSV (and passes Gate-2 once wh-562's validator exists); ships in the
  vendor manifest (deps-scan is a consumer skill)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] a GENERICALLY-NAMED git-tracked OSV watchlist + loader merge (NOT campaign-specific)
- [ ] file path confirmed with wh-562 RQ-B before creation (write-lane decision honored)
- [ ] MALWARE-DB.md documents the human-gated, Gate-2-validated APPEND process + an entry template
- [ ] seeded with the current confirmed entries, each dated + sourced (data, not logic)
- [ ] adding a new entry needs NO code change (extensibility test passes)
- [ ] reconciles with wh-5es (one watchlist; this instantiates its RQ2 schema) + shares the Trivy entry with wh-562
- [ ] safe versions don't flag (relies on wh-4k9); tests green

## Rollback
`git revert` — remove the curated file + the merge.

## References
docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md + _supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md +
_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G2 + §9.1); malware_db.py:26,47; confine_self_writes.py:40; OSV format
(osv.dev); ADR-012, ADR-019; wh-5es (schema), wh-562 (Gate-2 + write-lane), wh-4k9 (prereq).

title: deps-scan S8: version-aware match (fix the name-only false-positive bomb)
description:
## Goal
Fix the S8 false-positive bomb. `signal_s8` (**supply_chain.py:754** — corrected from :744) matches a
dependency by NAME ONLY (`if d["name"] in malware_db`), ignoring the version — so a watchlist entry for a
*popular* compromised package (LiteLLM, @tanstack/*) would flag EVERY user of that package, not just the
bad versions. Make S8 version-aware so it only flags the actually-installed bad version.
(docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md RQ3; implements wh-5es RQ3; loop-leverage
audit G3 — twice-confirmed: is_known_bad is DEAD CODE with zero production callers.)

## Prerequisites
- `is_known_bad(name, version, db)` exists (malware_db.py:47-52), matches name+version (or `"*"`), and
  is NOT imported into supply_chain.py — the import must be added.
- **HARD PREREQ (groom-verified):** the adapters (parse_npm/parse_pypi) emit deps with `spec` (the
  manifest RANGE) and NO `version` key — `d.get("version")` reads a nonexistent key. The
  lockfile-RESOLVED version must be extracted FIRST.

## Steps (reordered 2026-06-09 — resolved-version extraction is step 1, it gates everything)
1. **Extract the LOCKFILE-RESOLVED version in the adapters** (greenfield: package-lock/pnpm-lock for
   npm; poetry.lock/uv.lock for pypi) onto the normalized dep. When only a manifest RANGE exists (no
   lockfile), only the wildcard `"*"` entry may flag — NEVER flag a specific-version entry on a range.
2. **signal_s8:** add the is_known_bad import; replace the name-only check with
   `is_known_bad(d["name"], <resolved version>, malware_db)` — preserving the `"*"` wildcard.
3. **Update the TWO existing tests that pin the OLD name-only behavior** (they WILL break — TDD: encode
   the new intent with ==/!= pairs per Policy 9: safe-version+bad-entry → NO flag; bad version → flag;
   wildcard → flag).
4. Keep S8's degrade (no db → `[]`) + the pure-function / no-dep / no-network contract.

## Files to Modify
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py (adapters' resolved-version extraction + signal_s8 + the import)
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/ (the 2 pinned tests + new cases)

## Verification
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q` green (baseline: 78 passed, serial)
- popular pkg at a SAFE resolved version + a bad-version watchlist entry → S8 does NOT flag (FP fixed)
- same pkg at the BAD resolved version → S8 flags; a wildcard `"*"` entry still flags any version
- range-only (no lockfile) + specific-version entry → does NOT flag

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] adapters extract the lockfile-resolved version; range-only never produces a specific-version FP
- [ ] signal_s8 calls is_known_bad (imported) with the resolved version; name-only match removed
- [ ] the 2 old name-only tests updated + new tests pin: safe → no flag; bad → flag; wildcard → flag; range-only → no specific-version flag
- [ ] S8 still degrades (no db → []); pure function, stdlib-only

## Rollback
`git revert` — additive change to the matching logic.

## Notes
PREREQ for wh-k6l (seeding popular packages into the watchlist). Implements wh-5es RQ3 (delegated here);
ecosystem-key disambiguation stays OUT (the wh-5es RQ2 schema work). Smallest, highest-confidence win —
do FIRST in the wave (loop-leverage §8 sequence step 1).

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md (RQ3);
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G3/LBC-1 — adversarially confirmed);
supply_chain.py:754 (name-only), :975,:995-996 (consumption); malware_db.py:47-52; wh-5es, wh-k6l.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-09 (wave-1a pass, supersedes the earlier note — all claims re-verified): baseline GREEN (78 passed, serial). Confirmed: name-only @ supply_chain.py:754; is_known_bad @ malware_db.py:47-52 with ZERO non-test callers; NOT imported into supply_chain.py; adapters emit 'spec' only; lockfiles used ONLY for lockfile_present presence booleans (npm :236, pypi :407, gem :452, go :520) — resolved-version extraction IS greenfield (line 385 is the poetry MANIFEST dict form, not the lockfile). THE BREAKING TESTS, named precisely: (1) test_s8_offline_smoke.py::test_s8_name_match_fires_regardless_of_version (:125-136) pins the OLD intent verbatim — INVERT it (new intent: 9.9.9 not-in {1.2.3} must NOT flag); (2) test_malware_db.py::test_signal_s8_flags_dep_present_in_loaded_db (:151) dep spec '1.0.0' vs fixture {'1.0.0'} — breaks ONLY under a strict lockfile-only rule. OPEN IMPL DECISION (state it in the diff): an EXACT manifest pin (spec '1.0.0', no lockfile) is NOT a range — treat it as resolved (then test 2 + the smoke happy-paths stay green and only test 1 inverts). RECOMMENDED: exact-pin counts as resolved; range-without-lockfile = wildcard-only. Also fix in passing (file already in scope): stale 'supply_chain.py:348' ref in test_malware_db.py:4 docstring. Wildcard test test_supply_chain.py:314 unaffected.
close_reason: Closed

title: Interim quarantine (stopgap before wh-nvk's permanent removal): demote Trivy in the iac SCANNER_PREFERENCE (checkov leads) + strengthen the registry caveat
description:
## Goal
**Fail-safe off the TeamPCP-compromised Trivy NOW** (CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23) behind the
existing capability interface (ADR-015), degrading to the floor (ADR-003). Hours of work, gated only by
the lock test — decoupled from the spikes. **REFRAMED 2026-06-09: this is the STOPGAP BEFORE PERMANENT
REMOVAL** — wh-nvk's drop-Trivy decision superseded the original "demoted pending the integrity gate /
returns when cleared" framing. Trivy does not come back; wh-nvk's impl deletes/replaces what this edits.

## The ONE real edit (groom-verified — the original multi-step scope collapsed)
- SCA demotion is a NO-OP: Trivy is ALREADY last in the sca list (detect_tools.py:113-114).
- grype is NOT in SCANNER_PREFERENCE (prose-only in tool-registry.md:30) — not a code anchor.
- **THE edit: detect_tools.py:116 iac list — move ('trivy','*') AFTER ('checkov','*')** (Trivy currently
  leads iac).
- test_detect_tools.py:100-108 (category_tool['iac']=='trivy' under _which_only('trivy')) STAYS GREEN —
  no test edit needed.
- The lock test does NOT enforce order (capability-presence + the 0.7[01] version regex only) — keep a
  v0.70/v0.71 token in the registry caveat so test_registry_lock.py:51 stays green; doc-order parity is
  Policy 11, not lock-forced.

## Steps
1. detect_tools.py:116 — reorder the iac tuple list (checkov before trivy).
2. tool-registry.md — match the order (Policy 11 parity) + strengthen the Trivy caveat: cite
   CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23, the malicious set (binary v0.69.4, images v0.69.5/.6,
   force-pushed action tags 76/77), keep a v0.70/v0.71 token for the lock regex, and state "quarantined
   as the stopgap; permanent removal + replacement via wh-nvk".
3. Nothing else (surgical, Policy 3).

## Verification
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts/tests/test_registry_lock.py -q` green
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/tests -q` green (incl. the untouched iac test)
- checkov leads iac in SCANNER_PREFERENCE; floor degradation still works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] detect_tools.py:116 iac order: checkov before trivy; NO other order changes
- [ ] tool-registry.md order matches + the caveat cites the CVE/GHSA + keeps a v0.70/v0.71 token (lock green)
- [ ] caveat says stopgap-before-permanent-removal (wh-nvk), NOT "returns when cleared"
- [ ] both test suites green; commit cites the CVE + ADR-003/006/015

## Rollback
**No return path for Trivy** — permanent removal lands via wh-nvk; `git revert` this edit ONLY if wh-nvk
is abandoned entirely.

## References
CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23; docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md (RQ1/RQ2
FINAL); detect_tools.py:113-116, tool-registry.md:50-53, test_registry_lock.py:51,
test_detect_tools.py:100-108; ADR-003/006/015; wh-nvk (the permanent removal — supersedes the return path).
notes: GROOM 2026-06-09 (wave-1a pass — all claims re-verified against live code): baselines GREEN (sec-detect 62 passed; registry lock 4 passed; serial, nice -n 10). Confirmed: sca list trivy ALREADY last (detect_tools.py:113-114 — no-op, stay away); THE edit = :116 iac list, move ('trivy','*') after ('checkov','*'); test_detect_tools.py:100-108 iac test STAYS GREEN under the reorder (_which_only('trivy') → checkov absent → trivy still selected); lock test enforces capability-presence + the 0\.7[01] regex only, NOT order — keep a v0.70/v0.71 token in the caveat; CVE-2026-33634/GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23 confirmed ABSENT from tool-registry.md (0 hits — the caveat addition is real work); the IaC capability section is at tool-registry.md:36 (### IaC / container / CI) and the Trivy caveat block at :50-53. NEIGHBOR FINDING (out of scope, Policy 3 — do NOT bundle): the iac preference still lists hadolint (GPL-3.0, a wh-xn0 Gate-1 violator) at detect_tools.py:116 — owned by the wh-xn0/wh-nvk registry rewrite, not this stopgap. READY.

title: Spike: Gate-2 — deterministic DATA gate for watchlist/registry edits (per-entry GHSA/OSV provenance + OSV-schema + write-lane); Trivy/TeamPCP RQ1-RQ2 FINAL
description:
> **RE-SCOPED 2026-06-09** per the loop-leverage audit (docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md
> §4.1 + §5.1/LBC-6). The original RQ1 (verify) + RQ2 (exposure) are **FINAL** in
> docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md — do not re-research. The original "binary
> checksum-verify is OUR load-bearing control" framing was CORRECTED: the OSSF snapshot pin is ALREADY
> SHA-verified in code (docker/deps-scan-sandbox/fetch-snapshot.sh:16 40-hex enforce, :34 clone, :36
> checkout, :37 `git rev-parse HEAD == PIN || exit 1`); ARTIFACT provenance (tool binaries/images:
> commit-SHA/digest/checksum + cosign verify) is wh-hxt.3's RQ4 (CONTAIN's admission arm). The
> Trivy-return/reversibility framing is DEAD (wh-nvk's drop decision). What remains — and what this spike
> now IS — is **Gate-2: the deterministic DATA gate for supply-chain watchlist/registry edits.**

## Problem statement

The outer loop's eval keep/revert gate (Gate-1) STRUCTURALLY cannot score a watchlist or tool-registry
DATA edit: evals/score.py:64-95 consumes only findings-vs-label.json, and no corpus case measures "did
adding compromised-package X help" — so a poisoned or wrong-version entry could never be REVERTed on
merit, and an eval-J KEEP earned by an unrelated KB change must never smuggle a DATA row through
(false-merit merge). Today: (a) load_malware_db (malware_db.py:26-44) is a pure offline loader that
never verifies an entry against a primary source; (b) NO watchlist-entry validator exists (validate_kb.py
gates KB entries only); (c) the watchlist's planned home (deps-scan/reference/) is NOT in
confine_self_writes ALLOW_SEGMENTS (:40), so the outer loop cannot even write it; (d) gate_kb_edit
demands a gate-verdict.json==KEEP for /_shared/reference/ writes — the wrong verdict kind for DATA.

## Research questions (each -> a decision)
- RQ-A — THE GATE-2 VALIDATOR: a deterministic script (no LLM/RNG — Rule 5) mirroring validate_kb.py
  that gates every watchlist/registry DATA entry on: (1) REQUIRED primary-source advisory URL (GHSA/OSV)
  per entry; (2) OSV-JSON schema validity against a pinned schema; (3) regression-green (the existing
  malware_db/deps-scan tests + the version-aware predicate exercised). Decide its home: deps-scan/scripts/
  (next to malware_db.py) vs _shared/scripts/ as a reusable DATA-gate (open decision §9.1 — leaning
  _shared if wh-hxt.4's registry rows use the same gate).
- RQ-B — THE WRITE-LANE: extend confine_self_writes ALLOW_SEGMENTS (or relocate the watchlist file) so
  the outer loop CAN write watchlist entries — under Gate-2, NOT ungated. Decide the verdict mechanism:
  Gate-2's checks mint the verdict file for DATA edits, or a parallel gate hook handles the watchlist
  path; NEVER reuse an eval-J KEEP for a DATA row. Coordinate the file path with wh-k6l BEFORE the file
  is created (so it doesn't move).
- RQ-C — ONE WATCHLIST SCHEMA (ownership settled 2026-06-09): the schema IS the OSV-superset — wh-5es
  RQ2 FINALIZES it (ecosystem + extension/target kinds), wh-k6l INSTANTIATES the concrete file, THIS
  spike's Gate-2 VALIDATES it. The original bespoke tool-keyed shape ({tool:{bad_versions,bad_digests}})
  is DROPPED — tools enter as OSV entries with target:tool, not a second format. One file/feed/updater.
- RQ-D — THE ADR: ONE watchlist-mechanism ADR (next-available number AT WRITE TIME — last written
  ADR-023; NOTHING is reserved) covering Gate-1 vs Gate-2 (which gate governs which edit kind), the
  shared OSV-superset schema, and the write-lane. wh-5es's planned monitoring ADR FOLDS IN here (one
  watchlist -> one ADR; its design feeds this). The ADR's Context should cite ADR-001/004/022 as the
  source of the single-gate framing it refines (their "gated by the eval keep-or-revert" wording is
  scoped to KB edits only — append-only clarification, never edit them). If wh-hxt.3's strategy ADR did
  not carry the ADR-021 tag-pin supersession, this ADR states it (exactly one of the two does).
- RQ-E — KB entry: AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 (the TeamPCP technique) is DELEGATED to wh-q86 (exists).

## What already exists (do NOT rebuild)
- Snapshot-pin verification: fetch-snapshot.sh (SHA-enforced clone+checkout+verify) — done.
- Interim quarantine: wh-d5b. Permanent removal + replacement set: wh-nvk. The curated watchlist file +
  loader-merge + append process: wh-k6l. The OSSF feeder into sec-kb-refresh: wh-5es. The KB entry: wh-q86.
  This spike delivers the GATE + the write-lane + the one ADR.

## Exit criteria
- Gate-2 validator decided (home + checks + verdict mechanism) with an impl follow-up ticket.
- The write-lane decision recorded (ALLOW_SEGMENTS extension vs file relocation), coordinated with wh-k6l.
- The ONE watchlist-mechanism ADR appended (next-free number at write time), folding in wh-5es's ADR
  deliverable and stating the Gate-1/Gate-2 split.
- No bespoke tool-keyed schema anywhere (OSV-superset only).

## Time box
~1 day (research is done; this is design + ADR + impl-ticket carving). BLOCKED BY wh-hxt.3 (the strategy
ADR lands first; this ADR builds on it).

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (§4.1 Gate-2, §5.1 LBC-6 correction, G2);
docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md (RQ1/RQ2 FINAL);
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md. evals/score.py:64-95; malware_db.py:26-44,47-52;
confine_self_writes.py:40; gate_kb_edit.py:18,40-49; validate_kb.py (the pattern). Siblings: wh-hxt.3

Target users (often corporate, with their own endpoint security) must not have their host frozen either. title: Cap CPU/IO fan-out in every .claude/agents/*.md (endpoint-security + core-saturation freeze)

Surgical: no identity/role/frontmatter/scope changes.

title: Supply-chain-malware floor — generic capability, npm adapter first
description: Add an OFFLINE, static heuristic 'supply-chain-malware floor' behind the existing SCA capability (ADR-015) to catch NOVEL malicious/typosquatted packages that CVE-based SCA (npm audit/OSV-by-CVE) structurally misses. Ecosystem-AGNOSTIC by design: a shared signal core + a per-ecosystem adapter that normalizes that ecosystem's manifest+lockfile+hooks. Ship the generic core + the npm adapter FIRST (npm incidents are critical now); PyPI/RubyGems/Go/Cargo/Maven are follow-on adapters behind the same interface. Emits low/medium-confidence tool_assisted:false candidates; NEVER blocks; triage+human decide. Grounded in spike-09 (S1-S8 + scoring + remediation ladder) and its F5 generalization.
design: Architecture: (1) pure-function signal core (Rule 5, stdlib-only) over a normalized struct {deps:[{name,spec,source_type}], lifecycle_scripts, lockfile_present, script_files}. (2) npm adapter parses package.json + package-lock/pnpm-lock/yarn.lock into that struct. (3) Signals S1 lifecycle-hook present, S2 non-registry source dep, S3 unpinned+no-lockfile, S4 typosquat vs reference/ allowlist, S5 homoglyph/separator confusion, S6 dangerous-API strings in install script, S7 obfuscation markers, S8 known-bad vs OPTIONAL offline OSSF malicious-packages snapshot (hook; degrades when absent). (4) Scoring: emit on any HIGH (S5/S6/S8) OR >=2 corroborating; lone S1/S3 informational only. (5) Output reuses normalize_deps.py finding shape + degradation.py (category supply-chain, owasp A06:2021, access_required unknown, verified static_review_only, tool_assisted false, capped confidence); recommendation maps to a spike-09 F4 remediation rung. (6) Allowlist + ecosystem map live in deps-scan/reference/ so /sec-kb-refresh extends them (ADR-015).
notes: UPDATE 2026-06-08: scope groomed + approved-to-exist, but user chose DOCS-ONLY for now — implementation deferred pending explicit greenlight. Do NOT write code until then. Scope/design/DoD above are ready to execute when unblocked.
close_reason: Wave 2 done (team: dev-wh-07w + dev-wh-b5b). TL re-ran gates green: deps-scan 29 pytest; demo scan validates ([]) with 2 HIGH supply-chain candidates (tool_assisted:false, kb_refs AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-001, S8 degrades to tools_unavailable:[malware-db]); module pure/static (Rule5/ADR-007); fixtures INERT (white-hacker audited); SKILL.md 135 lines; manifest OK; wh-b5b stale-path grep=0. Ownership respected. NOT committed (operator owns git).

title: Epic T-13: Phase-13 QA-flow completion & doc reconciliation
description:
## Goal / Problem
Close the open verification tiers in docs/qa/20260607/qa-flows.md and reconcile the QA docs with the post-Phase-12/T-12.9 state (J=1.0, n=115). Beads is the source of truth; docs/plan/phase-13-qa-flow-completion.md is the human overview.
## Success Metrics
- Every QA-flow VC box is PASS or explicitly gated
- Each Wave-B flow emits a dated docs/qa/<date>/ verdict
## Scope
**In scope:** QA-1/3/4/6/7 deterministic verdicts + doc reconcile; gated live tiers tracked.
**Out of scope:** new product features; the opus re-baseline (T-12.5) and multi-file corpus (T-12.9b) are cross-phase carries.
## Execution Waves
**Wave A — DONE (closed):**
- `wh-4ym.1` T-13.1 reconcile qa-flows.md *(doc)*

- `wh-4ym.3` T-13.3 QA-6 CI-contract verdict *(verify)*

Launch a wave with `/launch-team <wave ids…>`. Cross-phase carries (not children): T-12.5 opus re-baseline, T-12.9b multi-file corpus.
## Acceptance Criteria

Per-package `uv run --project <pkg> --with pytest pytest <pkg>/tests -q`; `validate_manifest.py`; `claude plugin validate`. (NOT ruff/mypy/src/coverage — this repo doesn't use them.)

title: ATT&CK/ATLAS finding field + sec-report coverage matrix + structured disputed-claim field [supply_chain.py lane]
description:
## Goal (3 coupled parts, in dependency order)
(1) Add optional att_ck[] / atlas[] (or one xref[]) to finding-schema.json \$defs/finding (NOTE finding-schema.json:39 is additionalProperties:false — the field MUST be added explicitly) and populate it in the kb_refs matcher (sec-triage + deps-scan/scripts/normalize_deps.py + supply_chain.py) by copying the matched KB entry's xref[] subset.
(2) Add a PURE-aggregation script under sec-report/scripts/ (no LLM — Policy 5) that joins findings -> kb_refs -> KB xref[], groups by ATT&CK/ATLAS id, and emits a Technique|ID|covered?|modality|finding-refs matrix into SECURITY-REPORT.md; an empty cell (KB technique in scope, zero covering finding) is flagged a recall/degradation gap /sec-learn can harvest; reuse score.py's by_category grouping.
(3) Add optional disputed:{claim, dispute_source, status:enum[unresolved,refuted,confirmed]} to finding-schema.json + kb-entry-schema.json; matcher/triage propagate it; optionally cap confidence when status==unresolved (the supply-chain-3.md wiper is the first migration case).
PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE: map each technique to the canonical MITRE ATLAS/ATT&CK id from the official matrix (don't invent ids); disputed.status must reflect the actual primary-source split (keep unresolved until a primary source resolves it). Matrix + disputed field are internal-derived — no external rule lift.

title: GitHub Actions secrets-exfil + OIDC-exchange workflow-lint (signal-not-block)
description:
## Goal
Deterministic workflow-lint over .github/workflows/*.yml (routed via sec-detect, which already scopes .github/workflows): (a) flag toJSON(secrets) whole-context serialization co-located with actions/upload-artifact or an outbound POST; (b) flag a release/publish job combining permissions.id-token:write + an OIDC token-exchange URL (npm/v1/oidc/token/exchange) + a publish step naming a hardcoded target package. Signal-not-block (toJSON(secrets) + trusted-publishing have rare legit uses); route to triage; frame 'provenance/OIDC present != safe'.
PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE: confirm the malicious action pin + named target package list vs the primary GitHub/npm advisory; named packages land as WATCHLIST rows (S8 catches a resolved hijacked version), NOT hardcoded greps; toJSON(secrets) + oidc/token/exchange are durable structural tells — verify the legit-matrix-use FP rate before tuning severity.

title: Static-source-vs-EDR scope boundary + host-IR advisory (docs/process)
description:
## Goal
Document the boundary between STATIC-source review and runtime/EDR/host indicators, and capture a host-IR recovery advisory — both DOCUMENTATION only (no floor-signal change).
(1) sec-threat-model (+ a clause in exclusion-rules.md/core-checklist.md): file-write-hash telemetry, live C2 network/DNS, and process-memory scraping (/proc/<pid>/mem) are runtime/EDR/host indicators OUT of static-source scope and must route to a host/CI check — never claimed as static coverage. Add a containment note: DNS-block is structurally insufficient vs a decentralized canister dead-drop (*.raw.icp0.io, canister-id ending -cai) — egress-allowlist is the only lever.
(2) Append a host-IR recovery note to supply-chain-3.md (tagged 'host-level — advisory only'): isolate -> confirm daemon dead -> revoke from another host -> don't power off (preserve memory); LaunchAgent + systemd-user removal-before-rotate; rotation scope (GitHub PAT -> npm publish tokens + registry-log audit -> AWS Secrets Mgr all regions); immutable-config trap (chattr +i / chflags uchg); exfil-repo discovery.

## PRIMARY-SOURCE GATE (Policy 7)
IR ordering comes from a single DISPUTED community repo — adopt isolate-FIRST as primary; any third-party 'safe-revocation endpoint' is C2-shaped and AGAINST our no-egress posture — do NOT recommend it (one cautionary footnote max). Verify the canister host pattern + runtime IOCs vs a primary source; label UA/60s-poll low-durability. NOTE: the watchlist IOC-TTL idea is DEFERRED (no infra/C2 target kind exists yet — nothing to expire); record as design intent only.

## Files (File Ownership)
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-threat-model/SKILL.md (scope-boundary note)
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-3.md (advisory recovery note) [SHARED w/ T8 -> serialize]
- MODIFY plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/reference/core-checklist.md (1-line) [SHARED w/ T8,T11 -> serialize]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] grep -q 'EDR' / 'static-source' scope statement in sec-threat-model ; supply-chain-3.md carries the 'advisory only' recovery block ; no third-party revocation endpoint recommended
- [ ] uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .  → OK (docs-only, no code/signal change)

## Rollback
Revert the doc edits.

## References
