title: install.sh: offer to launch the interactive onboarding after vendoring (claude present → [Y/n]; else point to it)
description:
## Goal

After `vendor()` copies the payload (and the scrub), the installer **OFFERS to launch the interactive
onboarding** (the sec-init chat) so the user customizes the companion profile for THIS project. The
installer is `curl|bash` — it cannot chat itself — so it LAUNCHES a Claude Code session when possible,
and otherwise points the user to it. (User decision this session: "installer offers to launch it".)

## Prerequisites

- The interactive onboarding (sec-init chat) must EXIST first → **this ticket depends on the sec-init
  onboarding ticket**.
- `install.sh` conventions: function-wrapper (`main()` runs last), `set -euo pipefail`, the
  `log`/`warn`/`run` helpers, the `--unattended` + `--dry-run` flags, and **prompts read from
  `/dev/tty`** (stdin is the `curl` pipe, so `read </dev/tty`).

## Steps

1. After `vendor()` (post-scrub), add an onboarding handoff in the vendor lane:
   - if `--dry-run`: `log "[dry-run] would offer to run onboarding"`.
   - elif `claude` CLI present AND not `--unattended`: prompt on `/dev/tty`
     `"Run white-hacker onboarding now (detect stack + confirm the project profile)? [Y/n] "`; on
     yes → launch `claude "<onboarding prompt>"` interactively. The prompt instructs: *run the
     white-hacker sec-init onboarding — detect this project's stack (language, package manager,
     build/test) and confirm the `.white-hacker/project-profile.json` companion with me.*
   - else (claude absent / declined / `--unattended`): print the manual next-step (open Claude Code
     here and ask to onboard; it writes `.white-hacker/project-profile.json`).
2. Update the vendor-lane success message to mention the companion profile + onboarding.
3. Keep it **non-fatal**: a missing `claude`, a declined prompt, or a nonzero `claude` exit must NOT
   fail the install (`set -e` safe — guard with `|| true` / explicit branches).

## Files to Modify

- `install.sh` (the vendor-lane tail: onboarding handoff + the success message)

## Verification

- `bash -n install.sh` and `shellcheck -S warning install.sh` → clean
- `--unattended` run: NO prompt; prints the manual next-step
- `--dry-run` run: prints `[dry-run] would offer ... onboarding`
- with `claude` absent on PATH: prints the manual step, exits 0 (no error)
- the existing vendor smoke still passes (13 skills, 0 leaks): clone-local-main vendor into a temp target

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] onboarding handoff runs after `vendor()`+scrub; respects `--unattended` (no prompt) + `--dry-run`
- [ ] the prompt reads from `/dev/tty` (not the curl-pipe stdin); declining OR missing `claude` is
      non-fatal (exit 0)
- [ ] the launch prompt names sec-init + the `.white-hacker/project-profile.json` companion
- [ ] the success message mentions onboarding + the companion
- [ ] `bash -n` + `shellcheck` clean; the vendor smoke is unaffected

## Rollback

`git revert` the `install.sh` hunk (additive tail; nothing else depends on it).

## Notes

- User-decided design: installer OFFERS to launch (claude present → `[Y/n]`; else point-to-it); the
  customization is the COMPANION profile, never a forked agent md.
- Mirror the existing `ensure_uv` prompt pattern for the `/dev/tty` read + `--unattended` handling.

## References

ADR-017 (companion init), ADR-021 (vendor lane); `install.sh` `vendor()` + `main()` + the `ensure_uv`
`/dev/tty` prompt pattern; depends on the sec-init interactive-onboarding ticket.

title: sec-init: interactive onboarding chat + companion fields for package-manager + build/test
description:
## Goal

Turn `sec-init` from detect-and-write into an **interactive onboarding chat**: after detection it
CONFIRMS/CORRECTS the project facts WITH THE USER, then writes the refined project-scope companion
`.white-hacker/project-profile.json`. Capture the specifics the user called out — **package
manager(s)** and **build/test/lint commands** — as new factual companion fields. Stays a COMPANION
(ADR-017/ADR-004): never edits the shipped agent identity. (User decision this session: customization
lives in the companion, NOT a forked agent md — which also avoids the re-install clobber that hit rca.)

## Prerequisites

- ADR-017 (companion-not-rewrite) + ADR-004 (identity preservation) — unchanged.
- `sec-init` already imports detection from `sec-detect` (`detect_tools.build_scan_plan`) — reuse,
  don't reinvent (SKILL.md "Reuse, don't reinvent").
- The companion schema is `additionalProperties:false` + injection-safe (`is_factual` rejects
  imperatives) + the profile may feed `SessionStart` `additionalContext` (must stay < 8000 bytes).
  ALL of these invariants must still hold after the change.

## Steps

1. **Extend the project-profile schema** (sec-init's `project_profile_schema.json`) with NEW factual
   keys, keeping `additionalProperties:false`: `package_managers` (array of strings, e.g.
   `["uv","npm"]`), `build_test_commands` (object of factual command strings, e.g.
   `{"test":"uv run pytest","build":"...","lint":"..."}`), and optionally `in_scope_focus` (array of
   factual strings — project-specific concerns the user names). Values are FACTUAL (command strings
   like `uv run pytest` are factual statements, not imperatives).
2. **Detect package manager(s)** from manifests (extend detection): `package.json`+lockfile →
   npm/pnpm/yarn/bun; `pyproject.toml`/`uv.lock`/`poetry.lock`/`requirements.txt` → uv/poetry/pip;
   `go.mod` → go; `pom.xml`/`build.gradle` → maven/gradle. Seed `package_managers` + a best-guess
   `build_test_commands`.
3. **`init_profile.py` accepts REFINED inputs** (the user's confirmed/corrected values) so the agent
   can persist the user-confirmed profile — keep the deterministic `build_profile` for the detect-only
   seed and add a path to write a refined profile (still routed through `write_profile`, which already
   refuses non-factual / schema-invalid input).
4. **Rewrite `SKILL.md` to drive the interactive flow:** (a) run detection → show the user the
   detected langs/frameworks/package-managers/build-test + capabilities; (b) ASK the user to confirm
   or correct each, name any in-scope focus, and confirm the scoring standard (still human-confirmed,
   never inferred); (c) write the refined companion. Keep it ONE-time onboarding (not per-review).
5. **Tests:** schema accepts the new keys + rejects an identity-override key; `is_factual` accepts
   command strings (no false-reject); a refined profile with `package_managers`+`build_test_commands`
   validates and stays < 8000 bytes.

## Files to Modify

- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/SKILL.md` (the interactive confirm/correct flow)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts/init_profile.py` (detect package mgr; accept refined
  inputs; new fields)
- the sec-init project-profile schema JSON (new keys; `additionalProperties:false` preserved)
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts/tests/` (new tests)

## Verification

- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts/tests -q` → green
- a generated refined profile validates against the schema AND is < 8000 bytes
- schema still `additionalProperties:false`; an identity-override key (e.g. `posture`) is rejected
- vendor scrubber leakage guard stays green: `uv run --with pytest pytest install/tests/ -q`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] schema has `package_managers` + `build_test_commands` (+ optional `in_scope_focus`);
      `additionalProperties:false` preserved
- [ ] init detects package manager(s) from manifests for TS/Python/Go/Java
- [ ] `SKILL.md` describes the confirm/correct chat (detect → ask → write refined)
- [ ] `is_factual` accepts command strings; imperative/non-factual still rejected; `write_profile`
      refuses invalid
- [ ] profile stays < 8000 bytes (SessionStart cap); package tests green
- [ ] companion-only: shipped agent identity untouched (ADR-004/017)

## Rollback

`git revert` the sec-init change. The schema change is ADDITIVE (old profiles still validate), so no
data migration.

## Notes

- Design decided this session: companion (not agent-md fork); the installer-launch is the DEPENDENT
  follow-up ticket. Keep injection-safety (factual-only) — the profile may feed SessionStart.

## References

ADR-017 (companion), ADR-004 (identity preservation); `sec-init/SKILL.md` "Why a companion, not a
profile rewrite"; `docs/research/spike-07-agent-distribution-and-init-2026-06.md`; the installer-handoff
follow-up depends on this ticket.
close_reason: Closed

title: Spike: tool-admissibility — MIT/Apache-2.0-only license + local/no-telemetry data-egress gates; re-audit registry (→ next-available ADR)
description:
> **REFINED 2026-06-09:** the groom note is now folded into the body — the ADR number is DE-HARD-CODED
> everywhere (last written = ADR-023; nothing is reserved; whoever appends first takes the next free
> number — read docs/ARD.md's last header AT WRITE TIME). Trivy is STRUCK from the admitted survey
> digest (TeamPCP-compromised + dropped per wh-nvk — it must not appear as an admitted default). The
> "exactly ONE implementation ticket" exit is reconciled with wh-nvk (below).

## Research Question

Define + enforce a **two-gate admissibility policy for every capability TOOL**, then re-audit the
registry. (The GDPR *pipeline PII data-minimization* concern is a SEPARATE posture decision on disjoint
files: **Spike B = wh-81y**, its own next-free ADR. This ticket governs TOOLS, not the agent's output.)
Each RQ resolves to a decision:

- **RQ1 — Confirm the Gate-1 (license) violators; decide drop/replace for each.** Gate-1 = **MIT or
  Apache-2.0 ONLY**; reject BSD (incl BSD-3), LGPL, GPL, AGPL, MPL, any copyleft, proprietary,
  commercial. Verify each from its **upstream LICENSE file / SPDX**, not a blog. Seeded + verified
  (2026-06-09): Opengrep **LGPL-2.1**, Semgrep CE **LGPL-2.1**, CodeQL **proprietary**, govulncheck
  **BSD-3**, trufflehog **AGPL-3.0**, hadolint **GPL-3.0**, find-sec-bugs **LGPL**.
- **RQ2 — Per capability (SAST · SCA · secrets · IaC/CI · AI-redteam), name ≥1 admissible
  (MIT/Apache-2.0 + local + no-default-telemetry) tool, OR declare the floor the fallback (ADR-003).**
  SAST is the hard case: no permissive cross-language taint engine exists in 2026 → the honest answer is
  **floor + per-language Apache/MIT linters** (gosec, bandit, eslint-plugin-security Apache-2.0; ruff
  MIT; drop find-sec-bugs LGPL). **This is a precision DOWNGRADE from ADR-011's Opengrep default — the
  ADR must supersede ADR-011 explicitly, and the cost must be MEASURED against the eval corpus
  (evals/score.py) before KEEP, not asserted (Policy 9).**
- **RQ3 — Gate-2-egress (tool data-egress) verdict per admitted tool.** Runs **locally/offline**; does
  NOT upload source or send telemetry by default. Verify from docs/default config. Known: CodeQL = SaaS
  upload; **promptfoo telemetry ON by default** → admit only with `PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1`;
  trufflehog `--results=verified` makes live HTTP calls. (Naming note: this ticket's "data-egress gate"
  is DISTINCT from wh-562's "Gate-2" DATA-edit gate — different objects; keep the names apart in the ADR.)
- **RQ4 — Registry schema change.** Add `license` / `data_egress` / `gdpr` columns to
  `_shared/reference/tool-registry.md` so every entry carries its admissibility evidence, AND keep
  `detect_tools.py:110` `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` in sync (`test_registry_lock.py` enforces doc↔code at the
  CAPABILITY level; per-tool drops are enforced by the impl ticket's TDD, not the lock).
- **RQ5 — Tool-addition GATE wiring.** A **deterministic** two-gate screen (Policy 5):
  `license ∈ {MIT, Apache-2.0}` AND `data_egress == local && telemetry_off`, wired into the
  `sec-kb-refresh`/`sec-learn` tool-addition path (NOTE: that path is design-intent today — no
  registry-row writer exists; the screen lands as part of wh-hxt.4's ADMIT-via-loop writer, so SPEC it
  here, IMPLEMENT it there) — a NEW tool failing either gate is rejected with a stated reason.

## Timebox
Up to **1 week** (realistically 2–3 days).

## Background / Context
- **NG1** (PRD §2) — the product is the concept, not a fixed tool list; the registry's self-update is
  the design (ADR-015; proposer arm = wh-hxt.4). A license + data-egress admissibility gate is registry
  governance, not a coupling.
- **Precedent:** ADR-023 adopted MIT/Apache-2.0-only bundling and rejected psutil (BSD-3) — the bar is
  stricter than "permissive". This spike's ADR GENERALIZES that rule to every capability tool (append-only).
- **What's blocked:** tool-registry.md has no license/GDPR columns and seeds Gate-1 violators as
  defaults (Opengrep:24, govulncheck:29, trufflehog:34, hadolint:38; promptfoo/garak:42). ADR-011 names
  Opengrep (LGPL-2.1) the SAST default. Until the gates are codified, the loop can keep admitting
  non-compliant tools.
- **Binding ADRs (cite, don't re-debate):** ADR-015, ADR-006, ADR-003, ADR-023. ADR-011's tool choice is
  superseded by this spike's outcome.
- **Out of scope:** the agent's own model call + pipeline PII redaction → wh-81y.

## Investigation Approach
1. License verification (RQ1/RQ2): upstream LICENSE/SPDX per tool, record id + URL.
2. Data-flow verification (RQ3): local? uploads? telemetry default + disable flag? Pin flags.
3. SAST honest-answer (RQ2): floor + per-language linters; note emerging MIT SAST as track-via-refresh.
4. Schema + gate wiring (RQ4/RQ5): the columns + the deterministic screen SPEC (implemented in wh-hxt.4).
5. SAST-downgrade cost: the evals/score.py measurement plan before KEEP.

## Expected Deliverables
- [ ] Research report `docs/research/20260609_tool_admissibility_license_gdpr.md`.
- [ ] **An ADR at the NEXT AVAILABLE number at write time** (never pre-claim): the two gates; the
      re-audited admissible set; the registry column convention; the EXPLICIT ADR-011 supersession +
      the eval-corpus SAST-cost measurement plan.
- [ ] Per-capability admissibility matrix, every entry with verified SPDX + source URL.
- [ ] Impl follow-up: **the registry rewrite is SHARED with wh-nvk's impl** — one combined
      registry-rewrite ticket (columns from here + rows from wh-nvk + the lock-regex fix) or two
      explicitly file-sequenced tickets; never two uncoordinated writers to tool-registry.md +
      SCANNER_PREFERENCE. The deterministic two-gate screen SPEC hands to wh-hxt.4.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RQ1–RQ5 each answered → a stated decision; every license claim cited to upstream LICENSE/SPDX.
- [ ] Matrix fully populated; ≥1 admissible tool per capability OR the floor declared (the survey shows
      the floor fallback is NOT triggered — every capability has ≥1 admissible tool — but the policy
      names it).
- [ ] Data-egress verdict per admitted tool (telemetry/upload defaults + disable flags pinned).
- [ ] The ADR appended at the next-free number; ADR-011 supersession explicit; SAST cost measured not asserted.
- [ ] Registry-rewrite coordination with wh-nvk recorded (one combined ticket or explicit sequencing).
- [ ] No production code changed in this spike; timebox respected.

## Notes — pre-verified survey digest (2026-06-09; confirm, don't re-derive)
| Capability | Admitted MIT/Apache + local | Dropped violator (license) | Data-flow |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SAST | **floor** + gosec/bandit/eslint-plugin-security (Apache-2.0) + ruff (MIT) | Opengrep/Semgrep CE (LGPL-2.1), CodeQL (proprietary), find-sec-bugs (LGPL) | linters local; CodeQL = SaaS upload |
| SCA | OSV-Scanner/Grype/Syft/pip-audit (Apache-2.0), cargo-audit (MIT/Apache) | govulncheck (BSD-3); ~~Trivy~~ (Apache-2.0 but TeamPCP-compromised + dropped — wh-nvk; NOT admitted) | local |
| secrets | gitleaks (MIT), detect-secrets (Apache-2.0) | trufflehog (AGPL-3.0) | local; trufflehog --results=verified = live HTTP |
| IaC/CI | Checkov (Apache-2.0), actionlint/zizmor (MIT) | hadolint (GPL-3.0); ~~Trivy config~~ (dropped — wh-nvk) | local; Checkov offline w/o --bc-api-key |
| AI-redteam | promptfoo (MIT), garak (Apache-2.0) | — | promptfoo telemetry ON by default → PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1 |

## References
ADR-003/006/011/015/023; docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (§7 ADR-collision: nothing
reserved, next-free at write); wh-nvk (replacement set + the shared registry rewrite), wh-hxt.4 (the
tool-addition writer where the RQ5 screen lands), wh-81y (Spike B), wh-562 (naming: its Gate-2 is the
DATA-edit gate, distinct from this ticket's egress gate).
notes: GROOM 2026-06-10 (pre-claim, W-next pass; supersedes the 2026-06-09 note): VERDICT READY. (1) ADR NUMBERING UPDATE: ADR-024 is now TAKEN — wh-hxt.3's CONTAIN strategy ADR sits appended in the UNCOMMITTED tree (operator-gated). This ticket's ADR takes the next free number AT WRITE TIME (= ADR-025 if it writes next; wh-562 + wh-nvk also plan appends — serialize, never parallel-edit ARD.md). The body is clean (zero ADR-024 self-claims — the only occurrence was the old groom note). (2) ALL registry violator cites still EXACT despite wh-d5b's edits: Opengrep :24, govulncheck :29, trufflehog :34, hadolint :38, promptfoo/garak :42 (wh-d5b's changes were below :50). ADR-011 header at ARD.md:118; SCANNER_PREFERENCE at detect_tools.py:110; lock test GREEN (4 passed; capability-level only — per-tool enforcement is the impl ticket's TDD). (3) Trivy struck from the survey digest (~~Trivy~~ present); the registry-rewrite-shared-with-wh-nvk coordination stands. (4) RQ5's deterministic two-gate screen: SPEC here, IMPLEMENT in wh-hxt.4 (exists, blocked by wh-562 — correct). (5) NEW BINDING: ADR-024 ratified the artifact-provenance admission arm (its RQ4) — wh-xn0's integrity gate (d) should CITE ADR-024 rather than re-derive pinning policy; admissibility (license/egress) remains this ticket's own ground. NAMING CAUTION from the body stands: this ticket's 'Gate-2 data-egress' wording is DISTINCT from wh-562's 'Gate-2' DATA-edit gate — keep the names apart in the ADR.
close_reason: Closed

title: Spike: resource-aware execution — host probe + concurrency-cap mechanism (→ ADR-023)
description:
## Research Question

Decide HOW to back PRD §5.7 (FR-22..FR-27) + NFR-11 — the already-shipped *behavioral* "## Execution
budget" prose in `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md:145` — with a recorded decision. Each
question resolves to a Yes/No or a named choice:

- **RQ1 — Own script vs dependency?** Ship our own stdlib-only `resource_probe.py` (backlog wh-00i,
  under `_shared/scripts/`), take a permissive dep (psutil, BSD-3), or decide NO helper is needed and
  the shipped prose suffices (valid no-go)?
- **RQ2 — Per-OS probe commands?** Exact deterministic commands for cores / free-mem / load on macOS
  AND Linux (`os.cpu_count()` / `os.sched_getaffinity()` / `os.getloadavg()`; Linux `/proc/meminfo`
  MemAvailable; macOS `sysctl -n hw.memsize` + `vm_stat`).
- **RQ3 — Cap formula + constants?** Confirm/refine `min(cores − HEADROOM, free_mem ÷
  per_subagent_footprint, HARD_CEILING)` — the HEADROOM, HARD_CEILING, and the per-LLM-subagent
  footprint (the dominant fan-out cost).
- **RQ4 — Pressure signal, per-OS?** load-average vs PSI (cgroup v2 `memory.pressure`) vs MemAvailable
  — which triggers the drop to SEQUENTIAL, on Linux vs macOS?
- **RQ5 — Mode model?** Lock the four modes (ESSENTIALS/pre-commit · CRITICAL-ONLY · FULL · DEFERRED) —
  what each runs, the default-when-unsure, and how the probe *bounds* (not selects) them.
- **RQ6 — Output contract (RECOMMENDATION ONLY — see Scope).** Should resource posture become
  machine-visible (`mode` / `checks_skipped[]`) or stay report-only markdown? This is **NOT bound by
  ADR-023**: `_shared/reference/finding-schema.json` is `additionalProperties:false` (`:7,:12`) and is
  the keystone contract the eval gate (`_shared/scripts/validate_findings.py`, `evals/score.py`) +
  the baseline validate against — a new field is a BREAKING schema bump. The spike RECOMMENDS (default:
  report-only for now); if schema-visible is wanted it spawns its OWN follow-up ticket.

## Scope — one decision (validation FIX-1)

ADR-023 binds **RQ1–RQ5 only** — how the agent *measures and caps*. **RQ6 is a different axis on a
different contract**: a breaking change to the keystone `finding-schema.json` that ripples into
`validate_findings.py` + `evals/score.py` + the corpus + the (already-stale) baseline, and needs its
own eval-gate run. Folding it into a 1-day resource ADR would make this two decisions. Keeping RQ6 a
*recommendation* (not a binding) keeps this a single coherent spike (Policy 11 / ADR-009 artifact
contract).

## Timebox

**Timebox:** ~1 day (research + the ADR-023 write-up; **no production implementation**).

## Background / Context

The agent already ships behavioral guidance: `plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md:145`
§ "## Execution budget" tells it to probe `getconf _NPROCESSORS_ONLN || nproc || sysctl -n hw.ncpu`
for cores, `free -m`/`/proc/meminfo` or `sysctl hw.memsize`+`vm_stat`/`memory_pressure` for memory,
`uptime` for load, and to cap parallelism at `min(cores−2, free_mem ÷ footprint, ceiling)`, dropping
to SEQUENTIAL under pressure. PRD §5.7 elevates this to FR-22..27 + NFR-11 ("never OOM-freeze the
host") and §7 metric M8 ("0 host OOM-freezes; observed concurrency ≤ cap").

**Unresolved:** the *mechanism*. Keep parsing tool text by hand (Policy 5: a deterministic question
should be answered by code), or back it with a small helper. Backlog **wh-00i** already proposes
`resource_probe.py` (cores/free_mb/load1/suggested_max_parallel, stdlib-only, TDD) as a candidate
enhancer — PRD §8 scopes it as a candidate NOT a v1 requirement, and keeps OS-level *enforcement*
(cgroups/ulimit/nice) OUT (the cap is planned-and-honored, not kernel-enforced).

**Decisions blocked:** whether to build wh-00i and in what shape; the cap formula/constants; the
pressure signal; the mode contract; and (separately) the finding-schema question.

**Constraints (cite, do not re-debate):** ADR-003 (graceful degradation; never block on a missing
tool; the compute budget is continuous with tool-degradation, FR-13/FR-27 — SKIP ≠ PASS) ·
ADR-015 (capability floor / stdlib-first; add a port only when ≥2 tools implement it) · ADR-021
(every skill package is stdlib-only, `requires-python>=3.10`, zero runtime deps — "vendoring installs
ZERO Python packages into the target"; a new dep breaks this) · ADR-006 (pin-and-verify) ·
PRD §8 (OS-level enforcement out of scope) · License rule: **MIT or Apache-2.0 only**; reject
GPL/AGPL/copyleft/commercial.

## Investigation Approach

Survey is seeded (see Notes for verified facts); the spike CONFIRMS and decides. License-checked
candidates:

1. **Own stdlib helper (strong prior, wh-00i).** Verify cross-platform stdlib coverage:
   `os.cpu_count()` (all OS), `os.getloadavg()` (macOS+Linux, not Windows), `os.sched_getaffinity()`
   (Linux-only). Confirm the only piece needing a shell is free-mem: Linux read `/proc/meminfo`
   `MemAvailable` (pure Python); macOS `sysctl -n hw.memsize` + parse `vm_stat`. License: our code,
   Apache-2.0. Throwaway PoC under `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/` proving sane numbers on this
   macOS host AND (if reachable) a Linux container — TDD, runnable via `uv run --project`, mirroring
   the `_shared/scripts/` package shape.
2. **psutil dependency (BSD-3 — flag explicitly).** v7.2.2 (Jan 2026); `virtual_memory().available`
   cross-platform in one call. Evaluate ONLY as the "do we break the zero-dep rule for convenience?"
   alternative — BSD-3 is permissive but outside the MIT/Apache rule, and it breaks ADR-021's
   zero-Python-package vendor invariant.
3. **GNU `parallel` / `xargs -P` / ninja / bazel prior art (methodology, not a dep).** GNU parallel
   is **GPL-3** → cannot bundle; study its self-cap model as prior art only: `--jobs`=cores default,
   `--memfree` (no new job below N free; kill youngest below 50%), `--memsuspend`, `--load`. Key
   lesson: parallel's `--load` deliberately does NOT use load-average ("rises too slowly"). `xargs -P`
   (no mem awareness) + ninja/bazel (job/RAM pools) are weaker fits for orchestrating LLM subagents.
4. **Pressure-signal comparison.** PSI (cgroup v2 `memory.pressure`, kernel ≥4.20) predicts OOM
   before load rises but is Linux-only; macOS exposes `memory_pressure` (Yellow/Red) + compression/
   swap. Decide the per-OS trigger + the cross-platform fallback (MemAvailable / vm_stat).
5. **Free ≠ available subtlety.** Confirm the helper uses **MemAvailable not MemFree** on Linux
   (MemFree ignores reclaimable page cache) and accounts for macOS compressed/purgeable memory.
6. **Output-contract impact (RQ6, recommendation).** Read `_shared/reference/finding-schema.json`
   (`additionalProperties:false`) + `_shared/scripts/validate_findings.py`; recommend whether
   `mode`/`checks_skipped[]` become schema fields (eval-gate-visible, separate ticket) or stay
   report-only.

## Research Guidelines for AI Agents

```markdown
Please investigate and provide a clear research report with:

1. **Use Context7** for library/package documentation lookup (psutil, Python os/resource modules)
2. **Web search** for recent information (use current year 2026); VERIFY every candidate's LICENSE —
   MIT or Apache-2.0 only; reject GPL/AGPL/copyleft/commercial. Flag BSD/MPL explicitly.
3. **Structured findings** with a pros/cons table per option (license, maintenance, cross-platform
   coverage, dependency cost, degradation behavior)
4. **Clear recommendation** tied to ADR-003/015/021/006 + Policy 5; a NO-GO ("prose is enough") is a
   valid outcome ONLY if it records why deterministic measurement isn't needed (no silent SKIP→PASS)
5. **A throwaway PoC** under docs/research/poc-resource-probe/ if load-bearing for the decision
6. **Follow-up tasks** — ADR-023 spawns AT MOST TWO impl tickets (see Deliverables), or none on a no-go
```

## Expected Deliverables

- [ ] Research report at `docs/research/20260609_resource_aware_execution.md` (Findings template below).
- [ ] **DECISION appended to `docs/ARD.md` as ADR-023** covering **RQ1–RQ5**: own-script vs dep · exact
      per-OS probe commands · cap formula + HEADROOM + per-subagent footprint · pressure signal per-OS ·
      the four-mode model. RQ6 appears as a RECOMMENDATION, not a binding. A NO-GO ("shipped prose
      suffices") is equally valid **but must record WHY deterministic measurement isn't needed** (the
      inline probes don't silently degrade SKIP→PASS under ADR-003), not merely "prose exists".
- [ ] Throwaway PoC at `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/` only if load-bearing (TDD, `uv run --project`).
- [ ] **At most TWO follow-up tickets (validation FIX-2):** (a) wh-00i `resource_probe.py` (build,
      re-scoped by ADR-023); (b) ONLY if RQ6 lands schema-visible — a SEPARATE `finding-schema.json`
      bump ticket (its own eval-gate run). **NONE on a no-go.**

### Research Report File

```bash
touch docs/research/20260609_resource_aware_execution.md
```

## Findings Template

Copy this to `docs/research/20260609_resource_aware_execution.md`:

```markdown
# Research: Resource-aware execution — measuring host capacity + capping concurrency (no OOM-freeze)

**Date:** 2026-06-09
**Author:** <name>
**Spike Ticket:** <this-id>
**Status:** Draft | Final

## Summary
<one paragraph: the decision and why>

## Research Question
<RQ1..RQ6 above>

## Options Considered
| Option | License | Pros | Cons |
| ------ | ------- | ---- | ---- |
| Own stdlib resource_probe.py | Apache-2.0 (our code) | ... | ... |
| psutil dependency | BSD-3 (flag: not MIT/Apache) | ... | ... |
| GNU parallel / xargs prior-art | GPL-3 (cannot bundle) | ... | ... |
| Behavioral prose only (status quo) | n/a | ... | ... |

## Per-OS probe commands (RQ2)
| Signal | Linux | macOS |
| ------ | ----- | ----- |
| cores  | os.sched_getaffinity(0) / os.cpu_count() | os.cpu_count() / sysctl -n hw.ncpu |
| free   | /proc/meminfo MemAvailable | sysctl hw.memsize + vm_stat |
| load   | os.getloadavg() | os.getloadavg() |
| pressure | cgroup v2 memory.pressure (PSI) | memory_pressure (Yellow/Red) |

## Cap formula + constants (RQ3) / Pressure signal (RQ4) / Mode model (RQ5) / Output contract (RQ6)
<decisions; RQ6 as a recommendation>

## Recommendation
<winner + rationale tied to ADR-003/015/021/006 + Policy 5; or NO-GO with the why>

## References
- [Link](url) - Description

## Follow-up Tasks
- [ ] ADR-023 appended to docs/ARD.md (RQ1–RQ5)
- [ ] (if go) wh-00i implementation; (only if RQ6 schema-visible) a separate schema-bump ticket
```

### Create Follow-up Tickets

```bash
# After ADR-023 is recorded, spawn AT MOST TWO impl tickets (skip ALL on a no-go):
#  (a) re-scope + build the helper — backlog wh-00i (link, don't duplicate):
bd update wh-00i --description "...(re-scoped by ADR-023)..."
#  (b) ONLY if RQ6 decides schema-visible — a separate finding-schema bump:
# bd create "Add mode/checks_skipped[] to finding-schema.json (eval-gate run)" --type=task
```

- [ ] ADR-023 appended to `docs/ARD.md` (RQ1–RQ5)
- [ ] wh-00i re-scoped/linked (or explicitly none, on a no-go)
- [ ] (conditional) RQ6 schema ticket created only if schema-visible is chosen

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RQ1–RQ5 each resolved to a named choice / Yes-No, every claim source-cited (URL+version or
      repo file:line).
- [ ] RQ6 answered as a RECOMMENDATION with the schema-bump cost stated; if "yes", a SEPARATE ticket
      is the vehicle (not ADR-023, not wh-00i).
- [ ] Every candidate's LICENSE verified — only MIT/Apache-2.0 admissible as a bundleable dep;
      BSD (psutil) + copyleft (GNU parallel GPL-3) flagged + excluded.
- [ ] Principles evaluated: simplicity-first (Policy 2), graceful degradation + SKIP≠PASS (ADR-003),
      stdlib floor / zero-dep vendor (ADR-015, ADR-021), pin-and-verify (ADR-006); OS-enforcement
      stays out (PRD §8).
- [ ] **EXIT: ADR-023 appended to `docs/ARD.md`** (RQ1–RQ5) — or a recorded no-go WITH the why
      (no silent SKIP→PASS).
- [ ] Research report written to `docs/research/20260609_resource_aware_execution.md`.
- [ ] At most two implementation follow-ups created (or none on a no-go, with rationale).
- [ ] PoC (if any) under `docs/research/poc-resource-probe/` with passing `uv run pytest`.
- [ ] Timebox respected (~1 day) or extended with justification.

## Notes / Open Questions

Pre-verified survey facts (2026-06-09) — confirm, don't re-derive:
- **Stdlib coverage** supports the own-script prior: `os.cpu_count()` (all OS), `os.getloadavg()`
  (macOS+Linux), `os.sched_getaffinity()` (Linux-only). Only free-mem needs a shell.
  (cpython#105972)
- **GNU parallel is GPL-3** → prior-art/methodology only, never bundleable. (gnu.org/software/parallel)
- **Load-average is a lagging signal** — GNU parallel's own `--load` rejects it ("rises too slowly").
  (parallel_design.html)
- **PSI (cgroup v2 `memory.pressure`) beats load-average** for predicting OOM, but is Linux-only.
  (facebookmicrosites cgroup2 PSI)
- **Use MemAvailable, not MemFree** on Linux (MemFree ignores reclaimable page cache); macOS raw
  "Pages free" misleads — watch compression/swap + `memory_pressure` Yellow/Red. (Oracle blog)
- **psutil 7.2.2 (Jan 2026), BSD-3** — permissive but NOT MIT/Apache (flag it). (PyPI)
- **Per-LLM-subagent footprint is the dominant fan-out cost** — the cap's memory divisor must be the
  subagent footprint, not a generic process size.

Open questions for the spike to settle:
- What numeric per-subagent footprint estimate is safe/defensible (the divisor in RQ3)?
- Is PSI worth a Linux-only code path, or is MemAvailable a "good enough" single cross-platform signal?
- RQ6 schema decision: recommend report-only vs schema-visible; if schema-visible, it is a SEPARATE
  ticket (FIX-1), never folded into ADR-023 or wh-00i.
close_reason: DECIDED (ADR-023). GO: own stdlib resource_probe.py (reject psutil BSD-3 + GNU parallel GPL-3). PoC at docs/research/poc-resource-probe/ (stdlib-only, 10 tests, validated on macOS: 12c/10.4GB/load4.65->cap6). Report docs/research/20260609_resource_aware_execution.md. RQ1-5 bound ADR-023; RQ6=report-only (no schema bump). Follow-up: wh-00i (re-scoped, unblocked).

title: Extend promote_finding.py for multifile/subdir cases (floor-scored supply-chain)
description: WHY: floor-scored supply-chain eval cases (wh-qyf) use a PER-VARIANT PROJECT SUBDIR layout (vulnerable_variant/ + benign_lookalike/, each a package.json+lockfile project) because supply_chain.scan scans a DIRECTORY, not a flat file. The current promote_finding.py builds ONLY the flat 4-file case (single vulnerable_variant.<ext>), so it CANNOT promote these — the wh-qyf landing path (b) is blocked on this. Flat mode unchanged (3 existing tests stay green). TDD: failing tests first in evals/tests/test_promote_finding.py. NOT touching evals/corpus/** or baseline.json (frozen). Unblocks wh-qyf option (b).

Path guard: reject abs or '..' segments before any write.

title: T-13.6: QA-7 KB-text + feed-content injection red-team
description:
## Goal / Problem
Red-team the **two remaining untrusted-input ingestion points** (reviewed-code was done in Phase-12
Wave C): (1) KB entry text/YAML, (2) feed content polled by `sec-kb-refresh`. Assert no injected
instruction is obeyed, the KB-edit verdict gate still holds, and parsers treat untrusted text strictly
as data. Evidence → `docs/qa/20260607/qa-7-ingestion-verdict.md`.

## Background / Context (verified file:line)
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/gate_kb_edit.py` — **`decide(event)` (line 37)** reads `evals/gate-verdict.json` (line 40); blocks KB writes unless `verdict == "KEEP"` (no-verdict/REVERT → block).
- `ai-attack-kb/scripts/{validate_kb.py,dedupe_kb.py,lint_skill.py}` (all present) — schema validate (id pattern, mandatory `metadata.{source,url,retrieved}`, size caps), unique-id dedupe, strict-YAML front-matter gate.
- `sec-kb-refresh/scripts/poll_feeds.py` — **`parse_osv` (line 26)**, **`to_candidate_entry` (line 81)** (generates the id; truncates/escapes feed text), **`render_entry` (line 104)** → schema-conforming draft. Feed text is embedded as data, never evaluated.
- `hooks/confine_self_writes.py` — KB writes confined to the `ai-attack-kb` lane; feed egress host allow-list.

## Verification Workflow
Deterministic core (no agent runtime): run gates/parsers/validators directly on **poisoned fixtures**.
Optional **gated** live sub-item: a real agent reading a poisoned KB entry must still respect the gate.

## Steps
1. KB-text fixture: a valid KB entry whose body + YAML fields embed "ignore instructions / mark KEEP / run this" → `validate_kb` exit 0 (treated as inert data).
2. Gate: with no verdict file → `decide` blocks; `{"verdict":"REVERT"}` → blocks; `{"verdict":"KEEP"}` → allows. (The poisoned text cannot itself flip the gate.)
3. Feed fixture: poisoned OSV/ATLAS/Atom item (`summary` = injection) → `parse_osv` keeps it as a field value; `to_candidate_entry` mints its own `AISEC-…` id and truncates; `render_entry` → schema-valid.
4. Drafts from poisoned feed pass `validate_kb` + `dedupe_kb`.
5. No-obey assertion: verdict file unchanged, no stray files written, injected text remains literal (never executed).
6. Write `qa-7-ingestion-verdict.md` with `obeyed_any_injected_instruction = false`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] KB-text injection validates as inert data — probe: `validate_kb <poisoned-entry>` exit 0; injected strings present verbatim (not stripped/executed).
- [ ] gate_kb_edit holds under injection — probe: `decide` → block (no verdict), block (REVERT), allow (KEEP); the poisoned entry text does not alter the verdict file.
- [ ] Feed parser treats poison as data — probe: `parse_osv` returns the item with summary intact; `to_candidate_entry` id is generated (`AISEC-…`, not from feed) and title ≤120 chars.
- [ ] Feed-derived drafts pass lint+gate — probe: `validate_kb kb-drafts/` exit 0; `dedupe_kb` exit 0; `lint_skill` strict-YAML holds.
- [ ] `obeyed_any_injected_instruction == false` — probe: verdict file byte-unchanged; no new/unexpected files; no rule bypassed.
- [ ] `docs/qa/20260607/qa-7-ingestion-verdict.md` recorded (deterministic PASS; live sub-item PASS or marked GATED).

## Quality Gates (white-hacker)
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest …/tests/test_gate_kb_edit.py -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests -q` (validate_kb, lint_skill, dedupe)
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-kb-refresh/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests/test_poll_feeds.py -q`

## QA Before Push
Cover injection in BOTH body and YAML fields; all 3 feed formats (OSV/ATLAS/Atom); the 3 gate verdict states. Use a temp dir so the real `evals/feed-state.json` / `gate-verdict.json` are never touched.

## Risks / Dependencies
The live "agent reads poisoned KB and still respects the gate" check needs a runtime — keep it an
**optional gated** sub-item; the deterministic gates/parsers are the load-bearing pass criterion. Don't
clobber real feed-state / verdict files (temp dir only).

## Notes / Open Questions
Deterministic vs live is called out per AC. Existing tests (`test_gate_kb_edit`, `test_poll_feeds`,
`test_lint_skill`, `test_validate_kb`) already cover the happy path; this ticket adds the **poisoned**
fixtures and records the adversarial verdict.

---
close_reason: Closed

title: T-13.5: QA-4 security-policy verdict (present/absent/malicious + F1-F3)
description:
## Goal / Problem
Record a dated verdict that the **security-policy** subsystem treats SECURITY.md/security.txt as
**untrusted data**: absent→advisory (no severity/cvss), present→merged draft preserving maintainer
facts, malicious→injection flagged with **no body echo**, output confined to `PATCHES/`; and the F1–F3
hardenings + the scope-never-suppresses rule hold. Evidence → `docs/qa/20260607/qa-4-verdict.md`.

## Background / Context (verified file:line)
- `_shared/scripts/policy_detect.py` — facts-only extraction; **`read_capped(..., limit=_READ_LIMIT=256 KiB)` (line 70)** (F2 read cap); ReDoS-bounded email regex.
- `sec-policy/scripts/parse_policy.py` — gap report (booleans/enums/section names only); injection denylist returns a BOOLEAN, never echoes the matched span.
- `sec-policy/scripts/propose_policy.py` — **F1: `_safe_write` opens with `os.O_NOFOLLOW` (line 154)** → ELOOP refuse on symlink; PATCHES symlink + `..`/separator confinement guards; existing body preserved verbatim as a prefix.
- `sec-policy/scripts/hygiene_advisory.py` — **`is_factual` (line 44)** guards the advisory message (F3); advisory carries no `severity`/`cvss`/`owasp`.
- `sec-report/scripts/policy_scope.py` — **"NEVER drops a finding and NEVER changes/lowers severity" (line 8)**, `annotate_findings_with_scope` (line 20) adds only `out_of_scope_per_policy` (ADR-018).

## Verification Workflow
Investigate → build 3 SECURITY.md fixtures (absent / present-minimal / malicious-with-injection) → run the detect→parse→propose→advisory→scope chain + the F1/F2/F3 probes → record verdict. TDD if any new test is added.

## Steps
1. Baseline: run sec-policy / _shared / sec-report unit suites (must be green).
2. ABSENT fixture: `present=False`; `propose` action=`create` into `PATCHES/`; hygiene advisory fires (factual, no severity/cvss).
3. PRESENT fixture: `present=True`; `propose` action=`modify`; merged file starts with the original body verbatim; missing sections appended; maintainer email/versions/scope unchanged.
4. MALICIOUS fixture: `injection_suspected=True`; serialized gap-report JSON contains **zero** occurrences of the injected spans.
5. F1: symlinked output file AND symlinked PATCHES dir each → `ValueError` ("symlink"); real target byte-unchanged; nothing escapes.
6. F2: `detect_reporting_channel` on two 200K+ adversarial inputs each < 1.0s; `read_capped` enforces ≤256 KiB.
7. F3: `is_factual(advisory_message)` true; imperative variants false.
8. Scope: `annotate_findings_with_scope([HIGH], terms)` keeps severity+cvss, only adds the annotation.
9. Write `qa-4-verdict.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Absent→advisory (no severity/cvss) — probe: fixture run asserts `present=False`, advisory `kind=hygiene-advisory`, no `severity`/`cvss`.
- [ ] Present→modify, verbatim preservation — probe: merged text `.startswith(original)`; appended sections present; contact preserved.
- [ ] Malicious→flagged, no echo — probe: `injection_suspected=True`; `grep -c` injected spans in gap JSON → 0.
- [ ] F1 symlink write-escape refused (file + dir) — probe: both `propose()` calls raise `ValueError`; target unchanged; `pytest …/sec-policy/scripts/tests -k symlink -q` exit 0.
- [ ] F2 ReDoS bounded + read cap — probe: adversarial inputs < 1.0s; `read_capped` ≤262144 bytes; `pytest -k "redos or read_capped" -q` exit 0.
- [ ] F3 advisory factual — probe: `is_factual(msg)` True; imperatives False.
- [ ] Scope never suppresses a HIGH — probe: severity/cvss unchanged, only `out_of_scope_per_policy` added.
- [ ] Output confined to `PATCHES/` — probe: `propose` writes only under `PATCHES/`; `..`/separator names rejected.
- [ ] `docs/qa/20260607/qa-4-verdict.md` recorded.

## Quality Gates (white-hacker)
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-policy/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/_shared/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests -q`

## QA Before Push

## Risks / Dependencies
`O_NOFOLLOW` is best-effort on non-Unix; the `_assert_not_symlink` pre-check is the cross-platform guard — note the Windows caveat in the verdict. Prefer `uv run pytest` over ad-hoc `sys.path` scripts.

## Notes / Open Questions

title: T-13.4: QA-3 distribution+onboarding verdict (tier-2 + F-001)
description:
## Goal / Problem
Record a dated PASS/FAIL verdict that the **distribution + onboarding** contract holds deterministically:
the manifest validator enforces the canonical layout, `init_profile` writes a schema-valid
`security_policy`-bearing profile, the SessionStart **F-001 allowlist** drops injected prose, and the
plugin carries **no identity file** (posture lives only in the agent). Evidence → `docs/qa/20260607/qa-3-verdict.md`.

## Background / Context (verified file:line)
- `packaging/validate_manifest.py` — CLI `python validate_manifest.py [repo]`, exit 0 valid / 1 invalid; rejects unknown top-level keys (T-12.7).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts/init_profile.py` — writes `<repo>/.white-hacker/project-profile.json`; schema `project_profile_schema.json` (verified present) with `additionalProperties:false` (blocks any planted identity key); refuses to write non-factual values.
- `plugins/white-hacker/hooks/sessionstart_project_facts.py` — **F-001 allowlist**: `_ALLOWED_TOKEN = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9 ._/+-]{1,64}$")` (**line 52**), `_MAX_WORDS` (line 53), separator-aware `_WORD_SEP_RE` (defeats packed imperatives). Registered via `hooks/hooks.json` (SessionStart).
- Identity carriage: **no `CLAUDE.md` anywhere under `plugins/white-hacker/`** (verified); posture lives in `agents/white-hacker.md` (kind=agent, tools scoped, model=opus).

## Verification Workflow (investigate → probe → record)
Existing suites already pin behavior (sec-init, hooks, packaging). This ticket *runs them as a flow* on
real + crafted-malicious fixtures and records the verdict. New malicious-profile fixture is throwaway (`/tmp`).

## Steps
1. Manifest: run validator + `claude plugin validate` on the repo.
2. init_profile: run the CLI on a fixture repo; jsonschema-validate the written profile; confirm the `security_policy` block (7 keys).
3. SessionStart F-001: feed a crafted profile (markdown role-framing + imperative + `attacker.com` in `frameworks`/`path`/`reporting_channel`) and confirm none of it appears in `additionalContext`.
4. Identity carriage: `find … -iname CLAUDE.md` empty; agent.md declares posture/tools/model.
5. Write `qa-3-verdict.md` (commands, outputs, exit codes, commit hash, PASS/FAIL).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest valid — probe: `uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py . ; echo $?` → `0` + "OK"; `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker` → ✔.
- [ ] init_profile writes a schema-valid profile carrying `security_policy` — probe: run CLI on a fixture; `jq .security_policy <profile>` shows all 7 keys; jsonschema validates.
- [ ] SessionStart drops a crafted malicious profile — probe: pipe the fixture event; `grep -c '# SYSTEM\|developer mode\|attacker.com\|ignore all instructions'` on the emitted context → `0`; context still `is_factual`.
- [ ] F-001 allowlist holds + legit tokens survive — probe: `pytest …/hooks/tests/test_sessionstart_facts.py -k "f001 or f3" -q` → exit 0 (out-of-vocab/markdown/packed dropped; `fastapi`/`lang-python`/`github-pvr` kept).
- [ ] Identity carriage — probe: `find plugins/white-hacker -iname CLAUDE.md` empty; `grep -E '^(name|kind|tools|model):' plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md` all present.
- [ ] `docs/qa/20260607/qa-3-verdict.md` recorded (PASS/FAIL + commit hash).

## Quality Gates (white-hacker)
- `uv run --project packaging --with pytest pytest packaging/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-init/scripts --with pytest pytest …/tests -q`
- `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/hooks --with pytest pytest …/tests/test_sessionstart_facts.py -q`
- `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker`

## QA Before Push
Malicious-profile fixture exercises markdown / OOV-imperative / packed-separator / per-field cases; confirm legit single-token values still render (no over-redaction). Clean `/tmp` fixtures.

## Risks / Dependencies
`O_NOFOLLOW`/path specifics are F-001-adjacent only here. Needs `uv` + `claude` CLI on PATH (subscription; no API key). Capture `git rev-parse HEAD` for the verdict.

## Notes / Open Questions
The two closed-vocabulary fields (`security_policy.path`, `reporting_channel`) use exact-set matching (stricter than the shape allowlist) — verify both render only canonical values.

---

title: sec-detect AI-pass under-triggers: root-manifest-only detection → ai_pass:false on nested/agent-infra repos (dogfood F-008)
description:
## Goal / Problem
From the Gate-2 DOGFOOD self-review (docs/qa/20260611/dogfood/SECURITY-REPORT.md, F-008 / "G-extra"): the
product's OWN `sec-detect` (`detect_tools.py`), run from repo root, returns **`ai_pass:false`** on THIS
13-package Python AI-security codebase — because AI detection is root-manifest-only and the manifests are
nested (none at the repo root declares an AI SDK). **A naive self-scan would SKIP the AI pass entirely** — the
AI-security tool failing to recognize AI-agent infrastructure as such. Real coverage gap (no sink → informational
in this run, exclusion-rule 14, but a true product weakness for any monorepo / nested-package / agent-config repo).

## Scope (fix options)
- **Bounded recursive manifest discovery:** scan N levels (cap depth + exclude .venv/node_modules/.git) for AI
  SDK markers, not just root — so a nested `pyproject.toml`/`package.json` naming an AI SDK triggers the AI pass.
- OR an **"AI pass may be under-triggered" advisory:** when root AI-detection is empty BUT the tree contains
  Python/JS + AI/agent signals (`.claude/`, `agents/`, `mcp.json`, `*.skill`, an AI SDK in a nested manifest),
  emit a SCAN-PLAN advisory recommending the AI pass rather than silently `ai_pass:false`.
- Cite the existing ai-llm.md §9 (the config-poisoning detection just added) as a signal: a repo with `.claude/`
  agent config IS AI infrastructure even if no SDK is in a manifest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A repo with a NESTED AI-SDK manifest (or `.claude/` agent infra) yields `ai_pass:true` OR a stated advisory.
- [ ] Bounded discovery excludes `.venv`/`node_modules`/`.git`/build output (resource discipline).
- [ ] `detect_tools.py` tests pin: root-only AI repo → ai_pass:true (unchanged); nested-AI repo → ai_pass:true (was false).
- [ ] sec-detect package suite green.

## Notes
Found by dogfooding the product on itself (the AI-security tool didn't recognize its own AI-agent infra). Refs:
docs/qa/20260611/dogfood/SECURITY-REPORT.md F-008; `sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py:207`; `ai-llm.md §9`.

title: KB schema: add typed campaign_family lineage field (appended ADR + schema + validate_kb + backfill -002/-003) — deferred from wh-hxt.10
description:
## Goal / Problem
DEFERRED from the wh-hxt.10 groom (2026-06-10). The Hades RCA (RC3) wants campaign LINEAGE to be queryable —
Shai-Hulud → Mini Shai-Hulud → Hades/Miasma is one evolving family, and a static per-entry IOC list + 90-day
review_by can't track it. wh-hxt.10 ships AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-003 with lineage as PROSE + an xref, because
`campaign_family` does NOT exist in the closed KB schema (`kb-entry-schema.json` top-level
`additionalProperties:false`) — adding it FAILS validate_kb today, and per ADR-019 (docs/ARD.md:247 "the
closed vocabulary grows by an appended ADR, never silently") a closed-schema field addition needs an APPENDED
ADR, not an inline edit. This ticket adds the typed field properly.

## Scope (Files to Modify)
- `docs/ARD.md` — an APPENDED ADR (next-free number at write time): "KB entries carry an optional typed
  `campaign_family` field for lineage tracking" — the ADR-019-shaped decision.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/kb-entry-schema.json` — add `campaign_family` (optional string/enum).
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts/validate_kb.py` + tests — accept + validate the field.
- `plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/SKILL.md` — mirror the schema doc.
- Backfill `campaign_family: shai-hulud` into AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 + -003 (the prose lineage becomes typed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An appended ADR records the field decision (ADR-019 precedent shape).
- [ ] `kb-entry-schema.json` accepts `campaign_family`; validate_kb test pins BOTH a valid value AND that an
      entry without it still validates (optional). The KB suite stays green.
- [ ] SUPPLY-CHAIN-002/003 carry `campaign_family: shai-hulud`; a query/grep can enumerate the family.
- [ ] `additionalProperties:false` discipline preserved (the field is added to the schema, not bypassed).

## Notes
LOW priority — SUPPLY-CHAIN-003 (wh-hxt.10) ships fine with prose lineage meanwhile; this makes lineage a typed,
queryable field that the campaign-family re-poll trigger (folded into the wh-hxt.8 currency-arm impl) can key on.
Depends on wh-hxt.10 (the entries to backfill must exist). Groom before claiming. Refs: ADR-019 (docs/ARD.md:247),
wh-hxt.10 groom note, docs/research/20260610_hades_shai_hulud_pypi.md §5 RC3.

title: deps-scan S3: _unpinned matches literal 'x' anywhere — pypi specs embed the name, so 'xlrd==1.2.0' false-flags as an unpinned range
description:
## Goal / Problem
S3 ("unpinned ranges") false-positives on fully-pinned pypi deps whose NAME contains the letter x:
_unpinned's regex `[\^~*]|latest|x` matches a literal 'x' ANYWHERE, and the pypi adapter stores the
FULL PEP 508 line as spec (name embedded) — so 'xlrd==1.2.0' reads as "unpinned".

## Reproduction (commands)
cd plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts && nice -n 10 uv run --project . python -c "
import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.'); sys.path.insert(0,'../../_shared/scripts')
from supply_chain import _unpinned
print(_unpinned('xlrd==1.2.0'))   # True  <- the FP
print(_unpinned('boto3==1.0.0'))  # False <- control
"
End-to-end: a requirements.txt containing ONLY 'xlrd==1.2.0' (no lockfile) → scan() emits/feeds an S3
'unpinned ranges' signal for a fully-pinned dep.

## Expected vs Actual
Expected: a fully-pinned '==' dep is never "unpinned", regardless of its name. Actual:
_unpinned('xlrd==1.2.0') → True ('x' in the name matches the wildcard class).

## Suspected root cause
supply_chain.py _unpinned (~:733): the regex treats bare 'x' as a wildcard token without anchoring it
to a version segment, AND it receives the name-embedded pypi spec instead of the bare constraint.
PRE-EXISTING (not a wave-1a hunk — _unpinned was diff context in 0fa17a8); found by the wave-1a
verifier (workflow wsgfeszx6) while probing pypi spec shapes; filed separately per do-not-bundle.

## Files in scope
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py (_unpinned + its S3 call sites)
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/ (regression tests)

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
RED — the repro as failing tests → GREEN — fix → REFACTOR — align S3 with S8's constraint handling.

## Fix (either or both; surgical)
(a) feed _unpinned the name-stripped constraint — `_constraint(dep)` ALREADY EXISTS (added 0871c4f
    next to _exact_pin); pass `_constraint(d)` where S3 calls _unpinned (aligns S3 with S8);
(b) anchor the wildcard token: `[\^~]|\*|\blatest\b|(^|[.\s])x([.\s]|$)` so bare 'x' only matters as a
    version segment.

## Acceptance Criteria (regression tests)
- [ ] the FP class is dead AT THE CALL-SITE PATH: a requirements.txt 'xlrd==1.2.0' (no lockfile)
      produces no S3 signal from signal_s3 (:802) AND no per-dep corroborator hit (:1162). (Direct
      `_unpinned('xlrd==1.2.0') is False` asserts apply ONLY if fix (b) is chosen — under fix (a)
      _unpinned itself is unchanged, so assert through _constraint/the call sites instead.)
- [ ] _unpinned('1.x') / _unpinned('^1.0.0') / _unpinned('*') / _unpinned('latest') stay True (no FN)
- [ ] end-to-end: requirements.txt 'xlrd==1.2.0' (no lockfile) emits NO S3 signal; '^1.0.0' still does
- [ ] Package gate green (baseline 102), serial, nice -n 10, uv run --project

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
(white-hacker per-package gates — the repo's real gates, not the template's generic ruff/mypy example)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q` → green (baseline 102; serial, never `-n auto`)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` → OK

## QA Before Push
- [ ] Happy path: the ticket's probe cases pass end-to-end through `scan()` (not just unit helpers)
- [ ] Edge cases: empty/malformed/hostile manifest+lockfile inputs degrade, never raise
- [ ] Error handling: no new exception path; degrade contracts (`tools_unavailable`, empty struct) intact
- [ ] No regressions: the full deps-scan suite + the ==/!= pairs added by wh-4k9/0871c4f stay green

## Rollback
git revert — regex/call-site change + tests only.

## Commit Message Format
Repo rule (cite, don't restate): `fix(deps-scan): …` + `test:` for the RED commit; author `Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>`; NO AI attribution (CLAUDE.md Policy 12).

## Risks / Dependencies
- FILE OVERLAP: wh-7o1/wh-ln2/wh-0c2 also modify `supply_chain.py` — sequence, never the same wave.
- Fix (a) reuses `_constraint` (0871c4f) — if wh-7o1 lands first and reshapes constraint handling,
  rebase this on top (cheap).

## References
supply_chain.py (_unpinned, _constraint from 0871c4f, the pypi spec shape), the wave-1a verifier
finding (minor #3); sibling wh-7o1 (also touches pypi spec handling — coordinate, don't overlap files
in the same wave).

title: deps-scan: Gradle floor adapter — build.gradle/.kts + libs.versions.toml + gradle.lockfile (Java's other half has ZERO floor coverage)
description:
## Goal / Problem
A Gradle-only Java project gets ZERO dependency extraction on the stdlib floor: the deps-scan dispatch
table (supply_chain.py _DISPATCH) maps Java ONLY to pom.xml/parse_maven. sec-detect FINGERPRINTS
build.gradle/.kts (plugins/white-hacker/skills/sec-detect/scripts/detect_tools.py:36-37 (CROSS-PACKAGE — read-only context, not in this ticket scope)) and normalize_deps maps a 'gradle' label for TOOL-ASSISTED
output (deps-scan/scripts/normalize_deps.py:22) — so the tool-assisted path works when a scanner is installed (ADR-003
degrade is honest) — but the floor sees no deps, so S1-S8 (incl. the known-compromised watchlist) are
blind for the majority of modern Java + all Android.

## Background / Context
Precedent for pragmatic regex extraction of a code-shaped manifest: the Gemfile adapter (_GEM_RE).
Gradle build scripts are arbitrary code executed at build — also an S6/S7 surface. Naming contract:
"group:artifact" (matches OSV Maven ecosystem + parse_maven).

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
RED — fixture build.gradle/.kts + libs.versions.toml + gradle.lockfile cases failing through scan() →
GREEN — parse_gradle + dispatch entries → REFACTOR — share helpers with parse_maven where natural.

## Steps
1. parse_gradle: regex-extract implementation/api/compileOnly/runtimeOnly/testImplementation
   ("group:artifact:version") string coordinates + the map form (group:, name:, version:) from
   build.gradle AND build.gradle.kts. NOT a Groovy parser (the _GEM_RE precedent).
2. Version catalogs: gradle/libs.versions.toml via the defensive tomllib pattern; resolve libs.x refs
   where statically possible; else wildcard-only degrade.
3. gradle.lockfile (opt-in locking): parse 'group:artifact:version=configurations' lines as the
   RESOLVED source (mirrors package-lock; strongest signal).
4. Surface build.gradle/.kts via script_files so S6/S7 scan them (arbitrary build-time code).
5. Dispatch: add ("build.gradle", parse_gradle, "java", ...) + the .kts variant; never-raise/
   degrade-to-empty; settings.gradle multi-module = best-effort root only (document).

## Files to Create/Modify
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py (parse_gradle + dispatch entries)
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/ (new test module for the Gradle forms)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] string-coordinate, map-form, and .kts declarations extract name="group:artifact" (==/!= pairs)
- [ ] a known-bad coordinate in gradle.lockfile flags S8 at the locked version; a safe locked version
      does not (needs the wh-4k9 version-aware semantics — already shipped)
- [ ] a catalog-managed version resolves when the catalog defines it; unresolvable → wildcard-only
- [ ] build.gradle/.kts appear in script_files (S6/S7 coverage); no lifecycle false-claims
- [ ] malformed/hostile gradle files never raise (degrade to empty struct)
- [ ] Package gate green (baseline 102): nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
(white-hacker per-package gates — the repo's real gates, not the template's generic ruff/mypy example)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q` → green (baseline 102; serial, never `-n auto`)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` → OK

## QA Before Push
- [ ] Happy path: the ticket's probe cases pass end-to-end through `scan()` (not just unit helpers)
- [ ] Edge cases: empty/malformed/hostile manifest+lockfile inputs degrade, never raise
- [ ] Error handling: no new exception path; degrade contracts (`tools_unavailable`, empty struct) intact
- [ ] No regressions: the full deps-scan suite + the ==/!= pairs added by wh-4k9/0871c4f stay green

## Verification
AC probes as committed tests through scan(); plus one fixture mirroring a real Android-style module
(catalog + kts) to prove the floor produces value (ADR-003).

## Rollback
git revert — a new adapter + dispatch entries; no existing-adapter changes.

## Commit Message Format
Repo rule (cite, don't restate): `feat(deps-scan): …`; author `Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>`; NO AI attribution (CLAUDE.md Policy 12).

## Risks / Dependencies
- FILE OVERLAP: wh-7o1/wh-8ci/wh-ln2 also modify `supply_chain.py` — sequence, never the same wave.
- Regex extraction of Groovy/Kotlin DSL will miss exotic forms — ACCEPTED floor behavior (ADR-003:
  confidence-capped, tool-assisted path covers the rest); do not chase DSL completeness.
- Adds gradle script_files to the S6/S7 surface — wh-ln2's scrub (if landed first) must cover the new
  script_path values (it does by design: one helper at the builders).

## Notes
Storage/Schema: N/A. Out of scope: executing Gradle, parsing settings.gradle includes, Kotlin DSL
evaluation. Sequencing: independent of wh-7o1 (land either order).

## References
supply_chain.py (_DISPATCH dispatch, _GEM_RE precedent, parse_maven naming), detect_tools.py:36-37,
normalize_deps.py:22, ADR-003 (floor must produce value), wh-7o1 (version-extraction sibling), wh-k6l.

title: deps-scan: scrub control chars/newlines from attacker-controlled strings embedded in exploit_scenario (spec/s4_target/s5_target)
description:
## Goal / Problem
Findings text is an egress surface to the human reader, and supply_chain.py embeds attacker-controlled
manifest strings RAW into it: _make_finding (:1199; raw interpolation at :1206-1209) interpolates name, dep['spec'] and
target (s5_target or s4_target) into exploit_scenario — and the SAME class exists in the other two
scenario builders: _make_kernel_finding (:1047, interpolates build_file) and _make_script_finding
(:1236, interpolates script_path). PoC (wave-1a security review, PoC3): a spec of '\n\x1b[31mFAKE-CRITICAL' leaks a
newline + ANSI into the rendered report — a hostile manifest can visually forge severity lines.
PRE-EXISTING surface (wh-4k9's new `resolved` value never reaches finding text — PoC'd); filed
separately per do-not-bundle.

## Background / Context
The reviewed repo is untrusted input (Agents Rule of Two). No sanitization precedent exists in the
package (fold_name/unicodedata is S5 typosquat folding, not output escaping) — this ADDS the missing
render-time scrub at the single point of embedding.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
RED — tests pinning ==scrubbed AND !=raw → GREEN — a pure _scrub helper applied in _make_finding →
REFACTOR — one embedding path, no scattered escaping.

## Steps
1. Add a pure `_scrub(s: str) -> str`: strip C0/C1 control chars + ANSI CSI/OSC escapes, collapse
   newlines/tabs to a single space, cap length (~200 chars with an ellipsis marker).
2. Apply it in ALL THREE scenario builders — _make_finding (:1206), _make_kernel_finding (:1047),
   _make_script_finding (:1236) — to every repo-derived value interpolated into exploit_scenario
   (name, dep['spec'], target/s4_target/s5_target, build_file, script_path). One helper, one
   concern, three call sites — same render-time-scrub surface, NOT a bundle.
3. Tests: the PoC string renders inert; legitimate specs ('^1.0.0', '==1.2.3', git+https URLs) pass
   through unchanged (the != pair).

## Files to Create/Modify
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py (_scrub + _make_finding)
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/ (the PoC as a regression test)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] the PoC spec ('\n\x1b[31mFAKE-CRITICAL') cannot inject a newline or ANSI into exploit_scenario
      (== scrubbed) AND a benign spec is byte-identical after scrub (!= over-scrubbed)
- [ ] every repo-derived interpolation across the THREE scenario builders goes through _scrub
      (grep-assertable: no bare f-string use of name / dep['spec'] / target / build_file /
      script_path in _make_finding, _make_kernel_finding, _make_script_finding)
- [ ] findings stay schema-valid (validate_findings green in the package tests)
- [ ] Package gate green (baseline 102), serial, nice -n 10, uv run --project

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
(white-hacker per-package gates — the repo's real gates, not the template's generic ruff/mypy example)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q` → green (baseline 102; serial, never `-n auto`)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` → OK

## QA Before Push
- [ ] Happy path: the ticket's probe cases pass end-to-end through `scan()` (not just unit helpers)
- [ ] Edge cases: empty/malformed/hostile manifest+lockfile inputs degrade, never raise
- [ ] Error handling: no new exception path; degrade contracts (`tools_unavailable`, empty struct) intact
- [ ] No regressions: the full deps-scan suite + the ==/!= pairs added by wh-4k9/0871c4f stay green

## Verification
The AC probes as committed tests; plus re-run the security review's PoC3 one-liner and confirm inert.

## Rollback
git revert — additive helper + call-site changes.

## Commit Message Format
Repo rule (cite, don't restate): `fix(deps-scan): …`; author `Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>`; NO AI attribution (CLAUDE.md Policy 12).

## Risks / Dependencies
- FILE OVERLAP: wh-7o1/wh-8ci/wh-0c2 also modify `supply_chain.py` — sequence, never the same wave.
- Over-scrubbing risk: legitimate specs (git+https URLs, `^1.0.0`) must pass byte-identical — pinned
  by the != AC; findings must stay schema-valid (validate_findings in the package gate).
- If wh-0c2 lands first, its gradle script_path values are covered by the same builders — no extra work.

## Notes
Storage/Schema: N/A. Out of scope: report-renderer (sec-report) hardening — this fixes at the source.

## References
Wave-1a security review (info finding, PoC3); supply_chain.py _make_finding (:1199-1230), _make_kernel_finding (:1047), _make_script_finding (:1236);
Agents Rule of Two posture; ADR-019.

title: Tool-lifecycle codification: reusable retire→replace runbook + diversity-by-design registry policy (references wh-hxt.3's strategy ADR)
description:
## Goal
Codify the reusable RETIRE→REPLACE runbook + the DIVERSITY-BY-DESIGN registry policy. This ticket does
NOT append the strategy ADR — **wh-hxt.3 owns THE single strategy ADR** (CONTAIN-primary + the lifecycle
as defense-in-depth; the §9.3 ADR-collision decision, recorded 2026-06-09). This ticket REFERENCES that
ADR and turns two of its stages into operational artifacts. Append a new ADR ONLY if the runbook
mechanics genuinely need their own decision record (number computed at write time, never pre-claimed).

## Deliverable 1 — the reusable RETIRE→REPLACE runbook
Document the path: QUARANTINE (demote behind the capability interface; degrade to the floor — ADR-003)
→ SELECT a replacement (re-run the wh-xn0 ADMIT gates) → RECORD the decision → RETIRE (remove from
SCANNER_PREFERENCE + registry; handle the lock-test pin regexes). **Trivy is the worked first example:
wh-d5b (quarantine) → wh-nvk (select + permanent removal).** IMPORTANT correction (supersedes the
original framing): the quarantine stage is a STOPGAP BEFORE PERMANENT RETIREMENT, not a reversible
demotion — wh-nvk's drop-Trivy decision superseded wh-d5b's "returns when cleared" leg. The runbook must
state both exits: (a) retire permanently (the Trivy path), (b) restore only if the replacement
selection is abandoned. The next tool failure must be ROUTINE, not a fresh investigation.

## Deliverable 2 — DIVERSITY-BY-DESIGN as registry policy
A stated policy in tool-registry.md: >=2 vendors per high-value capability where a compliant second
source exists; cross-check sources (OSV-Scanner sanity-checks Grype). HONEST FRAMING (per the strategy +
the 2026 evidence): diversity is BLAST-RADIUS REDUCTION, not prevention — it is cited as the fix in zero
2026 postmortems; a backdoor you can't see hits all vendors equally; every added tool is another
supply-chain surface that must pass ADMIT + PIN+VERIFY + CONTAIN. Overlaps to reconcile: wh-nvk RQ3
validates the diversity thesis (cite its scorecard, don't re-derive); the policy text lands in
tool-registry.md — the same file wh-xn0 (columns) / wh-nvk (rows) / wh-d5b (caveat) / wh-hxt.1 (health
column) touch: SEQUENCE this ticket AFTER wh-nvk's registry rewrite to avoid a 5-writer collision.

## Dependencies (now wired in bd, not just prose)
- wh-hxt.3 — the strategy ADR this runbook cites (blocked-by).
- wh-nvk — the worked example completes there; and its registry rewrite must land before this ticket's
  policy text touches the same file (blocked-by).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The RETIRE→REPLACE runbook documented (quarantine→select→record→retire), Trivy worked through it
      as the example, with the stopgap-not-reversible correction and both exit paths stated
- [ ] Diversity-by-design stated as registry policy WITH the honest blast-radius-not-prevention framing
- [ ] References wh-hxt.3's ADR (no competing lifecycle ADR; a runbook-mechanics ADR only if genuinely
      needed, next-free number at write time)
- [ ] Reconciled with wh-nvk's diversity-thesis scorecard + sequenced after its registry rewrite

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (RETIRE + DIVERSIFY stages, the CONTAIN
reframe); docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (§9.3 ADR ownership); wh-hxt.3 (THE
strategy ADR), wh-d5b + wh-nvk (the worked example), wh-xn0 (ADMIT gates), wh-hxt.1 (health column);
ADR-003/015.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-10 (pre-claim, post wave-2-keystone + ADR-026/027/028 + wh-hxt.5/.6/.7 creation — corrections BIND): VERDICT READY, but WAVE MOVED 3→4. (1) KEY SEQUENCING (the body's own intent, now enforceable): Deliverable 2's policy text lands in tool-registry.md — the SAME file the registry rewrite wh-hxt.7 (created 2026-06-10) rewrites under the single-writer rule (ADR-025 §5/ADR-027 §5). bd dep WIRED 2026-06-10: wh-hxt.2 ← wh-hxt.7. The old 'sequence after wh-nvk's registry rewrite' pointed at the SPIKE (closed); the impl is wh-hxt.7. → wh-hxt.2 is NOT wave-3-parallel; it runs wave 4 (with wh-hxt.6/wh-hxt.1). (2) Cites resolve to RATIFIED ADRs now: 'wh-hxt.3's strategy ADR' = ADR-024 (CONTAIN primary; §1 demotes DIVERSIFY to blast-radius — the body's honest framing is now ADR text: cite ADR-024 §1 + ADR-027 §3, don't re-derive). The stopgap-not-reversible correction is FORMALIZED: ADR-027's Supersedes explicitly supersedes wh-d5b's temporary 'returns when cleared' framing — the runbook cites it. (3) The worked example is now COMPLETE end-to-end and richer than the body knew: QUARANTINE wh-d5b ✓ → SELECT the wh-nvk scorecard (RQ2/RQ3 — 5 dimensions; GO/EXTEND/REJECT) ✓ → RECORD ADR-027 ✓ → RETIRE mechanics = wh-hxt.7's steps (SCANNER_PREFERENCE drops + registry rows/Rejected subsections + the test_registry_lock.py:51 pin-regex retirement — the 'handle the lock-test pin regexes' step the body names is concretely specified there; cite, don't re-derive). (4) SELECT's ADMIT gates are now the ratified swap-criteria chain: admissibility ADR-025 (License-gate/Egress-gate) → the 5-dimension scorecard (wh-nvk report §2, incl. the AC#6 wrapper-shape check + AC#5 in-repo checksum-value pin for unsigned releases) → ADR-024 §5 admission (pin+verify) — diagrammed in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md §7.1 (the four-gates interaction + swap-criteria chain); the runbook codifies THAT chain. (5) NEW runbook step since the body was written: a tool retired FOR COMPROMISE also gets a Gate-2-validated watchlist entry (ADR-026; the Trivy entry is wh-k6l's instantiation, blocked on wh-hxt.5/.6 — the HARD ORDERING: gate_data_edit registered before the watchlist file exists). (6) Deliverable-1 home: a docs/ runbook (ADR-024 §7 precedent — operational guidance stays OUT of append-only ADR prose; the CI-hardening runbook from the contain-doc draft (c) is a SEPARATE artifact — do not merge them). 'A runbook-mechanics ADR only if genuinely needed' stands; last written = ADR-028, numbers at write time. (7) Deps state: wh-hxt.3 ✓ closed, wh-nvk ✓ closed, wh-hxt.7 OPEN (the new blocker). Scope OK: one runbook doc + (post-.7) one registry policy section; no overlap with wh-hxt.4's research doc.

title: Maintenance/staleness health: cadence/EOL gate on ADMIT + a staleness signal in the MONITOR feed
description:
## Goal
Add the MAINTENANCE/STALENESS health dimension to the tool lifecycle — the trivy-mcp lesson (an
unmaintained wrapper pinning a stale binary is a supply-chain risk distinct from compromise). THREE parts
(extended 2026-06-09 per loop-leverage G6 — RETIRE today has no tool/watchlist codepath):
(1) ADMIT gate — a tool must be actively maintained (release cadence, last-commit recency, NOT
EOL/feature-complete/archived); extends wh-xn0's admissibility gates + adds a registry health column.
(2) MONITOR signal — a staleness poller arm (latest-release age, archived/EOL/feature-complete flags via
the GitHub API) → proposes a human-gated swap BEFORE a tool is a liability (would have caught
gitleaks→Betterleaks and trivy-mcp). NOTE: this poller arm does NOT exist in code today —
poll_feeds.py:69 PARSERS={osv,atlas,atom}, no GitHub-release/staleness parser; this ticket DEFINES the
staleness signal; wh-5es owns the poller PLUMBING (one feed mechanism, don't fork it).
(3) RETIRE/SUPERSEDE paths (NEW — loop-leverage G6): today staleness_check.py:26-30,50-52 --archive ages
out KB entries ONLY — there is no codepath to retire a stale/compromised TOOL from
SCANNER_PREFERENCE/tool-registry, nor to supersede a WATCHLIST entry on a newer advisory (e.g.
gitleaks→Betterleaks). Add both: a tool-retire path (consumes the wh-hxt.2 runbook) and a
watchlist-entry supersede/remove path (in the shared OSV shape; Gate-2-validated).

## Notes
Deterministic where possible (a maintenance score from release/commit dates — Policy 5, not LLM judgment).
Human-gated proposals only (ADR-012, never auto-swap). Registry-file coordination: the health column
lands in tool-registry.md, which wh-xn0 (columns) / wh-nvk (rows) / wh-hxt.2 (policy) also edit —
sequence after wh-nvk's rewrite.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tool-registry gains a maintenance/health field; ADMIT rejects EOL/archived/feature-complete tools (or admits with a flagged caveat + a tracked successor, e.g. gitleaks→Betterleaks)
- [ ] a staleness signal DEFINITION (thresholds + the GitHub-API fields) handed to the wh-5es feed arm; poller plumbing not duplicated here
- [ ] a tool RETIRE path + a watchlist-entry SUPERSEDE path exist (extending staleness_check.py's KB-only --archive precedent), human-gated, Gate-2-validated for watchlist edits
- [ ] deterministic maintenance check (release/commit dates), not an LLM judgment
- [ ] reconciled with wh-xn0 (gates) + wh-5es (feed) + wh-hxt.2 (runbook); never auto-swaps

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (ADMIT + MONITOR + RETIRE stages);
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G6; staleness_check.py:26-30,50-52 is KB-only);
poll_feeds.py:69 (no staleness parser yet); wh-xn0, wh-5es, wh-hxt.2, wh-562 (Gate-2); ADR-012.
